For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Classifier policies?

Classifier policies: overview Classifier policies let a network administrator define sets of rules based on network traffic addressing or other header content, and use these rules to restrict or alter the passage of traffic through the switch. Choosing the rule criteria is called Classification, and one such rule, or list, is called a policy. Classification is achieved by creating a traffic class. The types of classes (IPv4, and IPv6) are each focused on relevant frame/ packet characteristics. Classes can be configured to match or ignore almost any frame or packet header field. Network traffic passing through a switch can be classified based on many different frame/packet characteristics including, but not limited to: •.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Frame ingress VLAN ID?

Frame ingress VLAN ID: • Source and/or destination IPv4, or IPv6 address • • Layer 2 (Ether Type) and Layer 3 (IP) protocol Layer 4 application ports A policy contains one or more policy entries, which are listed according to priority by sequence number. A single policy entry contains a class and corresponding policy action. Policy action is taken on traffic matched by its corresponding class. A policy can be applied to an individual front plane port, a Link Aggregation Group (LAG) interface, or a VLAN. IMPORTANT: See also ACL and Policy hardware resource considerations. Traffic policing Traffic policing supports policing of the inbound traffic. A typical application of traffic policing is to supervise the specification of traffic entering a network and limit it within a reasonable range. Another application is to "discipline" the extra traffic to prevent aggressive use of network resources by an application. For example, you can limit bandwidth for HTTP packets to less than 50% of the total.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the apply policy command do?

The apply policy command applies a policy to the global config context. Only one policy can be globally applied at a time. Applying a policy globally again, replaces the previous globally applied policy. The no form of this command removes application of the global policy. Syntax: apply policy <POLICY-NAME> in.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the apply policy command?
The syntax of the apply policy command is: apply policy <POLICY-NAME> in.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the class copy command do?

The class copy command copies a class to a new destination class or overwrites an existing class. Copying a class copies all entries as well. Syntax: class {ip|IPv6} <CLASS-NAME> copy <DESTINATION-CLASS>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the class copy command?
The syntax of the class copy command is: class {ip|IPv6} <CLASS-NAME> copy <DESTINATION-CLASS>

The syntax of the class reset command is: class { all | ip <CLASS-NAME> | IPv6 <CLASS-NAME>} reset.

The clear policy hitcounts command clears the policy hit count statistics.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the policy command do?

The policy command creates or modifies classifier policy and policy entries. A policy is made up of one or more policy entries ordered and prioritized by sequence numbers. Each entry has an IPv4/IPv6 class and zero or more policy actions associated with it. A policy must be applied using the apply command. The no form of the command can be used to delete either a policy (use no with the policy command) or an individual policy entry (use no with the sequence number). Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME>.

The syntax of the policy command is: policy <POLICY-NAME>

The policy copy command copies a policy to a new destination policy or overwrites an existing policy. Copying a policy copies all its entries as well. Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME> copy <DESTINATION-POLICY>.

The syntax of the policy copy command is: policy <POLICY-NAME> copy <DESTINATION-POLICY>

The policy resequence command resequences numbering in a policy. Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQ-NUM> <INCREMENT>.

The syntax of the policy resequence command is: policy <POLICY-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQ-NUM> <INCREMENT>

The policy reset command changes the user-specified policy configuration to match the active policy configuration. Use this command when a discrepancy exists between what the user configured and what is active and accepted by the system. Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME> reset.

section: ACL and Policy hardware resource considerations

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what restrictions or notes are documented for ACL and Policy hardware resource considerations?

For ACL and Policy hardware resource considerations, the guide documents the following restrictions, notes, or considerations: TCAM lookups There are four TCAM lookups available to use for these features. Each of these features uses one TCAM lookup when enabled. At most, four of these features can be enabled at the same time. Ingress Port IP ACL Ingress Port MAC ACL Ingress Port Policy Ingress Port Access Client Policy Ingress VLAN IP ACL Ingress VLAN MAC ACL Ingress VLAN Policy Ingress Global Policy.

topic: ACL and Policy hardware resource considerations

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Port Access Client Policy?

Port Access Client Policy: Matching precedence order When a packet is matched by multiple TCAM Lookups with the same action, a precedence order is followed. The precedence order from highest to lowest is as follows: Meter Actions: Port Access Client Policy Port Policy VLAN Policy.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Ingress Global Policy?

Ingress Global Policy: QoS Actions: Port Access Client Policy Remark Ingress Port Policy Remark Ingress VLAN Policy Remark Ingress Global Policy Remark QoS DSCP Map Entry QoS COS Map Entry QoS Port Config MAC Port ACL Logging IP Port ACL Logging MAC VLAN ACL Logging.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show resources command do?

The show resources command shows hardware resource consumption. Resource data is updated every 10 seconds. Hardware resource consumption information is shown for: • TCAM entries • TCAM lookups • Policers. Syntax: show resources.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show resources command?
The syntax of the show resources command is: show resources.

section: Introduction to the AOS-CX REST API

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Introduction to the AOS-CX REST API?

Introduction to the AOS-CX REST API: NOTE: The Aruba 6100 Switch Series only supports the default VRF and has no management port. Therefore, references in this guide to other VRFs or the management port do no apply to the 6100 Switch Series. Configuration for these switches should be done over an SVI having a physical port with access to the SVI, since the physical ports in the 6100 are not routed. AOS-CX REST API Switches running the AOS-CX software are fully programmable with a REST (REpresentational State Transfer) API, allowing easy integration with other devices both on premises and in the cloud. This programmability- combined with the Aruba Network Analytics Engine-accelerates network administrator understanding of and response to network issues. The AOS-CX REST API is a web service that performs operations on switch resources using HTTPS POST, GET, PUT, and DELETE methods. The AOS-CX REST API enables programmatic access to the AOS-CX configuration and state database at the heart of the switch.

topic: Introduction to the AOS-CX REST API

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Configuration attributes?

Configuration attributes: represent user-owned data. Although an attribute must be in the configuration category to be modified by a user, not all attributes in the configuration category can be modified after the resource instance is created. Configuration attributes that cannot be changed after the resource is created are called immutable attributes. This distinction matters when using a PUT request, because immutable attributes cannot be included in the request body. For example, a VLAN ID is an immutable attribute. You cannot change the ID of the VLAN after the VLAN is created. The VLAN name, in contrast, is a mutable (writable) attribute. You can change the VLAN name after the VLAN is created. Writable attributes Writable attributes are the subset of configuration attributes that are mutable. Writable attributes can be modified by a user after the resource is created. When using the PUT method to modify a resource, only writable attributes can be included in the request body.

section: Enabling access to the REST API

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what REST API endpoint is documented for POST /rest/v10.04/login? username=admin?

The REST API reference documents POST /rest/v10.04/login? username=admin. Details: The AOS-CX Web UI and AOS-CX real-time notifications subsystem rely on the REST API, therefore, all three are enabled or disabled together.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the https-server rest access-mode command do?
The https-server rest access-mode command changes the REST API access mode. The default mode is read-write. Syntax: https-server rest access-mode {read-only | read-write}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the https-server rest access-mode command?
The syntax of the https-server rest access-mode command is: https-server rest access-mode {read-only | read-write}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the https-server session close all command do?

The https-server session close all command invalidates and closes all HTTPS sessions. All existing Web UI and REST sessions are logged out and all real- time notification feature Web Socket connections are closed. Syntax: https-server session close all.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the https-server session close all command?
The syntax of the https-server session close all command is: https-server session close all.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the https-server vrf command do?

The https-server vrf command configures and starts the HTTPS server on the specified VRF. HTTPS server features include the REST API and the web user interfaces. The no form of the command stops any HTTPS servers running on the specified VRF and removes the HTTPS server configuration. Syntax: https-server vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the https-server vrf command?
The syntax of the https-server vrf command is: https-server vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show https-server command do?

The show https-server command shows the status and configuration of the HTTPS server. The REST API and web user interface are accessible only on VRFs that have the HTTPS server features configured. Syntax: show https-server [vsx-peer].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show https-server command?
The syntax of the show https-server command is: show https-server [vsx-peer]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about VLAN interfaces?

VLAN interfaces: • VLAN interfaces are of type vlan. • You can use the DELETE method to delete a VLAN interface. Example of creating a VLAN interface: Method and URI: POST "/rest/v10.04/system/interfaces" Request body: { "name": "vlan2", "vlan_tag": "/rest/v10.04/system/vlans/2", "vrf": "/rest/v10.04/system/vrfs/default", "type": "vlan" } Write methods (POST, PUT) supported in read-only mode The following switch resources support write methods (POST, PUT, or both) even when the REST API access mode is set to read-only:
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about About AOS-CX?

About AOS-CX: AOS-CX introduction AOS-CX is a new, modern, fully programmable operating system built using a database-centric design that ensures higher availability and dynamic software process changes for reduced downtime. In addition to robust hardware reliability, the AOS-CX operating system includes additional software elements not available with traditional systems, including: • Automated visibility to help IT organizations scale: The Aruba Network Analytics Engine allows IT to monitor and troubleshoot network, system, application, and security-related issues easily through simple scripts. This engine comes with a built-in time series database that enables customers and developers to create software modules that allow historical troubleshooting, as well as analysis of historical trends to predict and avoid future problems due to scale, security, and performance bottlenecks.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Initial configuration?

Initial configuration: Perform the initial configuration of a factory default switch using one of the following methods: • Load a switch configuration using zero-touch provisioning (ZTP). When ZTP is used, the configuration is loaded from a server automatically when the switch booted from the factory default configuration. • Connect the management port on the switch to your network, and then use SSH client software to reach the switch from a computer connected to the same network. This requires that a DHCP server is installed on the network. Configure switch settings and features by executing CLI commands. • Connect a computer running terminal emulation software to the console port on the switch. Configure switch settings and features by executing CLI commands. Initial configuration using ZTP Zero Touch Provisioning (ZTP) configures a switch automatically from a remote server. Prerequisites • The switch must be in the factory default configuration. Do not change the configuration of the switch from its factory default configuration in any way, including by setting the administrator password.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure banners?

To configure banners, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure the banner that is displayed when a user connects to a device using a console port or in-band 2. Configure the banner that is displayed after a user is authenticated. Use the command banner exec 3. Log in to the CLI 4. Switch to config context.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure the in-band management interface?

To configure the in-band management interface, follow the documented procedure: 1. Switch to the in-band management interface context with the command interface vlan 1 2. SSH is enabled by default on the default VRF. If disabled, enable SSH with the command ssh server. Commands shown in the procedure include: vrf default..
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp authentication command do?
The ntp authentication command enables support for authentication when communicating with an NTP server. The no form of this command disables authentication support. Syntax: ntp authentication.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp authentication command?
The syntax of the ntp authentication command is: ntp authentication.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp authentication-key command do?

The ntp authentication-key command defines an authentication key that is used to secure the exchange with an NTP time server. This command provides protection against accidentally synchronizing to a time source that is not trusted. The no form of this command removes an authentication key. Syntax: ntp authentication-key <KEY-ID> {md5 | sha1} <PASSWORD> [trusted].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp authentication-key command?
The syntax of the ntp authentication-key command is: ntp authentication-key <KEY-ID> {md5 | sha1} <PASSWORD> [trusted]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp disable command do?
The ntp disable command is documented in this guide. Syntax: ntp disable.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp disable command?
The syntax of the ntp disable command is: ntp disable.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp enable command do?

The ntp enable command enables the NTP client on the switch to automatically adjust the local time and date on the switch. The NTP client is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables the NTP client. Syntax: ntp enable.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp enable command?
The syntax of the ntp enable command is: ntp enable.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp server command do?

The ntp server command defines an NTP server to use for time synchronization, or updates the settings of an existing server with new values. Up to eight servers can be defined. The no form of this command removes a configured NTP server. Syntax: ntp server <IP-ADDR> [key <KEY-NUM>] [minpoll <MIN-NUM>] [maxpoll <MAX-NUM>][burst | iburst] [prefer] [version <VER-NUM>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp server command?
The syntax of the ntp server command is: ntp server <IP-ADDR> [key <KEY-NUM>] [minpoll <MIN-NUM>] [maxpoll <MAX-NUM>][burst | iburst] [prefer] [version <VER-NUM>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp trusted-key command do?

The ntp trusted-key command sets a key as trusted. When NTP authentication is enabled, the switch only synchronizes with time servers that transmit packets containing a trusted key. The no form of this command removes the trusted designation from a key. Syntax: ntp trusted-key <KEY-ID>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp trusted-key command?
The syntax of the ntp trusted-key command is: ntp trusted-key <KEY-ID>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp vrf command do?
The ntp vrf command nOTE: 6100 only supports default VRF. Specifies the VRF on which the NTP client communicates with an NTP server. 28 AOS-CX 10.06 Fundamentals Guide. Syntax: ntp vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp vrf command?
The syntax of the ntp vrf command is: ntp vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ntp associations command do?

The show ntp associations command shows the status of the connection to each NTP server. The following information is displayed for each server: • Tally code: The first character is the Tally code: ◦ ◦ ◦ ◦ (blank): No state information available (e.g. non-responding server) x: Out of tolerance (discarded by intersection algorithm).: Discarded by table overflow (not used) -: Out of tolerance (discarded by the cluster algorithm) ◦ +: Good and a preferred remote peer or server (included by the combine algorithm) ◦ #: Good remote peer or server, but not utilized (ready as a backup source) ◦ *: Remote peer or server presently used as a primary reference ◦ o: PPS peer (when the prefer peer is valid) • ID: Server number. • NAME: NTP server FQDN/IP address (Only the first 24 characters of the name are displayed). Syntax: show ntp associations.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp associations command?
The syntax of the show ntp associations command is: show ntp associations.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ntp authentication-keys command do?
The show ntp authentication-keys command shows the currently defined authentication keys. Syntax: show ntp authentication-keys.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp authentication-keys command?
The syntax of the show ntp authentication-keys command is: show ntp authentication-keys.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp servers command?
The syntax of the show ntp servers command is: show ntp servers.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ntp statistics command do?

The show ntp statistics command shows global NTP statistics. The following information is displayed: • Rx-pkts: Total NTP packets received. • Current Version Rx-pkts: Number of NTP packets that match the current NTP version. • Old Version Rx-pkts: Number of NTP packets that match the previous NTP version. • Error pkts: Packets dropped due to all other error reasons. • Auth-failed pkts: Packets dropped due to authentication failure. • Declined pkts: Packets denied access for any reason. • Restricted pkts: Packets dropped due to NTP access control. • Rate-limited pkts: Number of packets discarded due to rate limitation. • KOD pkts: Number of Kiss of Death packets sent. Syntax: show ntp statistics.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp statistics command?
The syntax of the show ntp statistics command is: show ntp statistics.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ntp status command do?
The show ntp status command shows the status of NTP on the switch. Syntax: show ntp status.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp status command?
The syntax of the show ntp status command is: show ntp status.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure a layer 2 interface?

To configure a layer 2 interface, follow the documented procedure: 1. Change to the interface configuration context for the interface with the command interface 2. Set the interface MTU (maximum transmission unit) with the command mtu 3. Review interface configuration settings with the command show interface.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface command do?

The interface command switches to the config-if context for a physical port. This is where you define the configuration settings for the logical interface associated with the physical port. Syntax: interface <PORT-NUM>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface command?
The syntax of the interface command is: interface <PORT-NUM>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no interface vlan <VLAN-ID> command do?

The no interface vlan <VLAN-ID> command creates an interface VLAN also know as an SVI (switched virtual interface) and changes to the config-if- vlan context. The specified VLAN must already be defined on the switch. The no form of this command deletes an interface VLAN.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip address command do?
The ip address command sets an IP/IPv6 address on the interface VLAN. The no form of this command removes the IP/IPv6 address from the interface. Syntax: ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [secondary].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip address command?
The syntax of the ip address command is: ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [secondary]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip mtu command do?

The ip mtu command sets the IP MTU (maximum transmission unit) for an interface. This defines the largest IP packet that can be sent or received by the interface. The no form of this command sets the IP MTU to the default value 1500. Syntax: ip mtu <VALUE>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip mtu command?
The syntax of the ip mtu command is: ip mtu <VALUE>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 address command?
The syntax of the IPv6 address command is: IPv6 address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>{eui64 | [tag <ID>]}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show allow-unsupported-transceiver command do?
The show allow-unsupported-transceiver command displays configuration and status of unsupported transceivers. Syntax: show allow-unsupported-transceiver.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show allow-unsupported-transceiver command?
The syntax of the show allow-unsupported-transceiver command is: show allow-unsupported-transceiver.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show interface command do?
The show interface command displays active configurations and operational status information for interfaces. Syntax: show interface [<IFNNAME>|<IFRANGE>] [brief | physical | extended [non-zero]].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface command?
The syntax of the show interface command is: show interface [<IFNNAME>|<IFRANGE>] [brief | physical | extended [non-zero]]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show interface dom command do?

The show interface dom command shows diagnostics information and alarm/warning flags for the optical transceivers (SFP, SFP+). This information is known as DOM (Digital Optical Monitoring). DOM information also consists of vendor determined thresholds which trigger high/low alarms and warning flags. Syntax: show interface [<INTERFACE-ID>] dom [detail].

The syntax of the show interface dom command is: show interface [<INTERFACE-ID>] dom [detail]

The show interface transceiver command is documented in this guide. Syntax: show interface [<INTERFACE-ID>] transceiver [detail | threshold-violations].

The syntax of the show interface transceiver command is: show interface [<INTERFACE-ID>] transceiver [detail | threshold-violations]

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the shutdown command do?
The shutdown command disables an interface. Interfaces are disabled by default when created. The no form of this command enables an interface. Syntax: shutdown.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the shutdown command?
The syntax of the shutdown command is: shutdown.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about VLANs?

VLANs: are primarily used to provide network segmentation at layer 2. VLANs enable the grouping of users by logical function instead of physical location. They make managing bandwidth usage within networks possible by: • Allowing grouping of high-bandwidth users on low-traffic segments • Organizing users from different LAN segments according to their need for common resources and individual protocols • Improving traffic control at the edge of networks by separating traffic of different protocol types. • Enhancing network security by creating subnets to control in-band access to specific network resources VLANs are generally assigned on an organizational basis rather than on a physical basis. For example, a network administrator could assign all workstations and servers used by a particular workgroup to the same VLAN, regardless of their physical locations. Hosts in the same VLAN can directly communicate with one another. A router or a Layer 3 switch is required for hosts in different VLANs to communicate with one another.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you create and enabling a VLAN?
To create and enabling a VLAN, follow the documented procedure: 1. Switch to the configuration context with the command config 2. Create a new VLAN with the command vlan.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you disable a VLAN?

To disable a VLAN, follow the documented procedure: 1. Switch to configuration context with the command config 2. Switch to configuration context for the VLAN you want to disable with the command vlan 3. Disable the VLAN with the command shutdown.

section: Assigning a VLAN to an interface

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Assigning a VLAN to an interface?

Assigning a VLAN to an interface: To use a VLAN, it must be assigned to an interface on the switch. VLANs can only be assigned to non-routed (layer 2) interfaces. All interfaces are routed (layer 3) by default when created. Use the no routing command to disable routing on an interface.

topic: Assigning a VLAN to an interface

section: Assigning a VLAN ID to an access interface

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Assigning a VLAN ID to an access interface?

Assigning a VLAN ID to an access interface: Prerequisites At least one defined VLAN. Procedure 1. Switch to configuration context with the command config. 2. Switch to the interface that you want to define as an access interface with the command interface. 3. Configure the access interface and assign a VLAN ID with the command vlan access. Examples.

topic: Assigning a VLAN ID to an access interface

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show capacities svi-count command do?
The show capacities svi-count command shows the maximum number of SVIs supported by the switch. Syntax: show capacities svi-count.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show capacities svi-count command?
The syntax of the show capacities svi-count command is: show capacities svi-count.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show vlan [<VLAN-ID>] command do?
The show vlan [<VLAN-ID>] command displays configuration information for all VLANs or a specific VLAN.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show vlan port command?
The syntax of the show vlan port command is: show vlan port <INTERFACE-ID>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show VLANsummary command do?
The show VLANsummary command displays a summary of the VLAN configuration on the switch. Syntax: show VLANsummary.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show VLANsummary command?
The syntax of the show VLANsummary command is: show VLANsummary.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan <VLAN-LIST> command do?

The no vlan <VLAN-LIST> command creates a VLAN and changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. By default, the VLAN is enabled. To disable a VLAN, use the no shutdown command. If the specified VLAN exists, this command changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. If a range of VLANs is specified, the context does not change. The no form of this command removes a VLAN. VLAN 1 is the default VLAN and cannot be deleted.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan access [<VLAN-ID>] command do?

The no vlan access [<VLAN-ID>] command creates an access interface and assigns an VLAN ID to it. Only one VLAN ID can be assigned to each access interface. VLANs can only be assigned to a non-routed (layer 2) interface or LAG interface. By default, all interfaces are routed (layer 3) when created. Use the no routing command to disable routing on an interface and change the interface to a layer 2 interface. The no form of this command removes an access VLAN from the interface in the current context and sets it to the default VLAN ID of 1.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan trunk allowed [<VLAN-LIST>] command do?

The no vlan trunk allowed [<VLAN-LIST>] command assigns a VLAN ID to an trunk interface. Multiple VLAN IDs can be assigned to a trunk interface. These VLAN IDs define which VLAN traffic is allowed across the trunk interface. VLANs can be assigned only to a non-routed (layer 2) interface or LAG interface. By default, all interfaces are routed (layer 3) when created. Use the no routing command to disable routing on an interface. The no form of this command removes one or more VLAN IDs from a trunk interface. When the last VLAN is removed from a trunk interface, the interface continues to operate in trunk mode, and will trunk all the VLANs currently defined on the switch, and any new VLANs defined in the future. To disable the trunk interface, use the command shutdown.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan trunk native [<VLAN-ID>] command do?

The no vlan trunk native [<VLAN-ID>] command assigns a native VLAN ID to a trunk interface. By default, VLAN ID 1 is assigned as the native VLAN ID for all trunk interfaces. VLANs can only be assigned to a non-routed (layer 2) interface or LAG interface. Only one VLAN ID can be assigned as the native VLAN. NOTE: When a native VLAN is defined, the switch automatically executes the vlan trunk allowed all command to ensure that the default VLAN is allowed on the trunk. To only allow specific VLANs on the trunk, issue the vlan trunk allowed command specifying only specific VLANs. The no form of this command removes a native VLAN from a trunk interface and assigns VLAN ID 1 as its native VLAN.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan trunk native <VLAN-ID> tag command do?

The no vlan trunk native <VLAN-ID> tag command enables tagging on a native VLAN. Only incoming packets that are tagged with the matching VLAN ID are accepted. Incoming packets that are untagged are dropped except for BPDUs. Egress packets are tagged. The no form of this command removes tagging on a native VLAN.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the voice command do?
The voice command configures a VLAN as a voice VLAN. The no form of this command removes voice configuration from a VLAN. Syntax: voice.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the voice command?
The syntax of the voice command is: voice.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Configuration and firmware management?

Configuration and firmware management: Checkpoints A checkpoint is a snapshot of the running configuration of a switch and its relevant metadata during the time of creation. Checkpoints can be used to apply the switch configuration stored within a checkpoint whenever needed, such as to revert to a previous, clean configuration. Checkpoints can be applied to other switches of the same platform. A switch is able to store multiple checkpoints. Checkpoint types The switch supports two types of checkpoints: • System generated checkpoints: The switch automatically generates a system checkpoint whenever a configuration change occurs. • User generated checkpoints: The administrator can manually generate a checkpoint whenever required. Maximum number of checkpoints • Maximum checkpoints: 64 (including the startup configuration) • Maximum user checkpoints: 32 • Maximum system checkpoints: 32 User generated checkpoints User checkpoints can be created at any time, as long as one configuration difference exists since the last checkpoint was created.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint auto command do?

The checkpoint auto command starts auto checkpoint mode. In auto checkpoint mode, the switch temporarily saves the runtime configuration as a checkpoint only for the specified time lapse interval. Configuration changes must be saved before the interval expires, otherwise the runtime configuration is restored from the temporary checkpoint. Syntax: checkpoint auto <TIME-LAPSE-INTERVAL>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint auto command?
The syntax of the checkpoint auto command is: checkpoint auto <TIME-LAPSE-INTERVAL>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint auto confirm command do?

The checkpoint auto confirm command signals to the switch to save the running configuration used during the auto checkpoint mode. This command also ends the auto checkpoint mode. Syntax: checkpoint auto confirm.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint auto confirm command?
The syntax of the checkpoint auto confirm command is: checkpoint auto confirm.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint diff command do?

The checkpoint diff command shows the difference in configuration between two configurations. Compare checkpoints, the running configuration, or the startup configuration. Syntax: checkpoint diff {<CHECKPOINT-NAME1> | running-config | startup-config}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint diff command?
The syntax of the checkpoint diff command is: checkpoint diff {<CHECKPOINT-NAME1> | running-config | startup-config}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint post-configuration timeout command?
The syntax of the checkpoint post-configuration timeout command is: checkpoint post-configuration timeout <TIMEOUT>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint rename command do?
The checkpoint rename command renames an existing checkpoint. Syntax: checkpoint rename <OLD-CHECKPOINT-NAME> <NEW-CHECKPOINT-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint rename command?
The syntax of the checkpoint rename command is: checkpoint rename <OLD-CHECKPOINT-NAME> <NEW-CHECKPOINT-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint rollback command do?

The checkpoint rollback command applies the configuration from a pre-existing checkpoint or the startup configuration to the running configuration. Syntax: checkpoint rollback {<CHECKPOINT-NAME> | startup-config}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint rollback command?
The syntax of the checkpoint rollback command is: checkpoint rollback {<CHECKPOINT-NAME> | startup-config}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> command do?

The copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> command copies a checkpoint configuration to a remote location as a file. The configuration is exported in checkpoint format, which includes switch configuration and relevant metadata. Syntax: copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> command?
The syntax of the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> command is: copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> {running-config startup-config} command?
The syntax of the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> {running-config startup-config} command is: copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> {running-config | startup-config}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL> command do?

The copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL> command copies an existing checkpoint configuration to a USB drive. The file format is defined when the checkpoint was created. Syntax: copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL> command?
The syntax of the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL> command is: copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command do?

The copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command copies a remote configuration file to a checkpoint. The remote configuration file must be in checkpoint format. Syntax: copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command?
The syntax of the copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command is: copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy <REMOTE-URL> {running-config startup-config} command?
The syntax of the copy <REMOTE-URL> {running-config startup-config} command is: copy <REMOTE-URL> {running-config | startup-config } [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy running-config {startup-config checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME>} command?
The syntax of the copy running-config {startup-config checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME>} command is: copy running-config {startup-config | checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME>}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy {running-config startup-config} <REMOTE-URL> command?
The syntax of the copy {running-config startup-config} <REMOTE-URL> command is: copy {running-config | startup-config} <REMOTE-URL> {cli | json} [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy {running-config startup-config} <STORAGE-URL> command?
The syntax of the copy {running-config startup-config} <STORAGE-URL> command is: copy {running-config | startup-config} <STORAGE-URL> {cli | json}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy startup-config running-config command do?
The copy startup-config running-config command copies the startup configuration to the running configuration. Syntax: copy startup-config running-config.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy startup-config running-config command?
The syntax of the copy startup-config running-config command is: copy startup-config running-config.

The syntax of the copy <STORAGE-URL> running-config command is: copy <STORAGE-URL> {running-config | startup-config | checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME>}.

The syntax of the erase {checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> startup-config all} command is: erase {checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> | startup-config | all}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command do?
The show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command shows the configuration of a checkpoint. Syntax: show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> [json].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command?
The syntax of the show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command is: show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> [json]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show checkpoint list command do?
The show checkpoint list command shows a list of saved checkpoints. Syntax: show checkpoint list {all | <START-DATE> <END-DATE>}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show checkpoint list command?
The syntax of the show checkpoint list command is: show checkpoint list {all | <START-DATE> <END-DATE>}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the boot set-default command do?
The boot set-default command sets the default operating system image to use when the system is booted. Syntax: boot set-default {primary | secondary}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the boot set-default command?
The syntax of the boot set-default command is: boot set-default {primary | secondary}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the boot system command do?

The boot system command reboots all modules on the switch. By default, the configured default operating system image is used. Optional parameters enable you to specify which system image to use for the reboot operation and for future reboot operations. Syntax: boot system [primary | secondary | serviceos].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the boot system command?
The syntax of the boot system command is: boot system [primary | secondary | serviceos]

The show boot-history command shows boot information. When no parameters are specified, shows the most recent information about the boot operation, and the three previous boot operations for the active management module. When the all parameter is specified, shows the boot information for the active management module. Syntax: show boot-history [all].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show boot-history command?
The syntax of the show boot-history command is: show boot-history [all]

The copy {primary secondary} <REMOTE-URL> command uploads a firmware image to a TFTP or SFTP server. Syntax: copy {primary | secondary} <REMOTE-URL> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].

The copy {primary secondary} <FIRMWARE-FILENAME> command copies a firmware image to USB storage. 100 AOS-CX 10.06 Fundamentals Guide. Syntax: copy {primary | secondary} <FIRMWARE-FILENAME>.

The copy primary secondary command copies the firmware image from the primary to the secondary location. Syntax: copy primary secondary.

The syntax of the copy primary secondary command is: copy primary secondary.

The copy <REMOTE-URL> command downloads and installs a firmware image from a TFTP or SFTP server. Syntax: copy <REMOTE-URL> {primary | secondary} [vrf <VRF-NAME>].

The syntax of the copy <REMOTE-URL> command is: copy <REMOTE-URL> {primary | secondary} [vrf <VRF-NAME>]

For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy <STORAGE-URL> command?
The syntax of the copy <STORAGE-URL> command is: copy <STORAGE-URL> {primary | secondary}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure SNMP?

To configure SNMP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable SNMP on a VRF using the command snmp-server vrf default 2. Set the system contact, location, and description for the switch with the following commands: 3. If required, change the default SNMP port on which the agent listens for requests with the command. Commands shown in the procedure include: snmp-server agent-port..
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the rmon alarm index <INDEX> snmp-oid <SNMP-OID> rising-threshold <RISING-THRESHOLD> command do?

The rmon alarm index <INDEX> snmp-oid <SNMP-OID> rising-threshold <RISING-THRESHOLD> command stores configuration entries in an alarm table that defines the sample interval, sample-type, and threshold parameters for an SNMP MIB object. Only the SNMP MIB objects that resolve to an ASN.1 primitive type of INTEGER (INTEGER, Integer32, Counter32, Counter64, Gauge32, or Time Ticks) will be monitored. The no form of this command removes all RMON alarms and allows you to specify an index to remove a particular RMON alarm. Syntax: rmon alarm index <INDEX> snmp-oid <SNMP-OID> rising-threshold <RISING-THRESHOLD>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the rmon alarm index <INDEX> snmp-oid <SNMP-OID> rising-threshold <RISING-THRESHOLD> command?
The syntax of the rmon alarm index <INDEX> snmp-oid <SNMP-OID> rising-threshold <RISING-THRESHOLD> command is: rmon alarm index <INDEX> snmp-oid <SNMP-OID> rising-threshold <RISING-THRESHOLD>

The syntax of the show rmon alarm command is: show rmon alarm [index <INDEX>]

For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmp trap command?

For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmp vrf command do?
The show snmp vrf command displays the VRF on which the SNMP agent service is running. Syntax: show snmp vrf.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmp vrf command?

The show snmpv3 context command displays all configured SNMP contexts. Syntax: show snmpv3 context.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 context command?

For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmpv3 engine-id command do?

The show snmpv3 engine-id command displays the configured SNMP v3 snmp engine-id. If the SNMP v3 engine-id is not configured, by default a unique engine-id is created by the switch using a combination of the enterprise OID value and the switch's MAC address. Syntax: show snmpv3 engine-id.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 engine-id command?
The syntax of the show snmpv3 engine-id command is: show snmpv3 engine-id.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmpv3 security-level command do?
The show snmpv3 security-level command displays the configured SNMP v3 security level. Syntax: show snmpv3 security-level.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 security-level command?
The syntax of the show snmpv3 security-level command is: show snmpv3 security-level.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmpv3 users command do?
The show snmpv3 users command displays all configured SNMP v3 users. For more details on the user enabled status, see snmpv3 security-level. Syntax: show snmpv3 users.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 users command?
The syntax of the show snmpv3 users command is: show snmpv3 users.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server agent-port command do?

The snmp-server agent-port command sets the UDP port number that the SNMP master agent uses to communicate. UDP port 161 is the default port. The no form of this command sets the SNMP master agent port to the default value. Syntax: snmp-server agent-port <PORT>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server agent-port command?
The syntax of the snmp-server agent-port command is: snmp-server agent-port <PORT>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server community command do?

The snmp-server community command adds an SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. A community string is a password that controls read access to the SNMP agent. A network management program must supply this name when attempting to get SNMP information from the switch. A maximum of 10 community strings are supported. Once you create your own community string, the default community string (public) is deleted. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. When no community string exists, a default community string with the value public is automatically defined. Syntax: snmp-server community <STRING>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server community command?
The syntax of the snmp-server community command is: snmp-server community <STRING>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server host command do?
The snmp-server host command is documented in this guide. Syntax: snmp-server host <IPv4-ADDR | IPv6-ADDR> trap version <VERSION> [community <STRING>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server host command?
The syntax of the snmp-server host command is: snmp-server host <IPv4-ADDR | IPv6-ADDR> trap version <VERSION> [community <STRING>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server system-contact command do?
The snmp-server system-contact command sets SNMP contact information. The no form of this command removes the SNMP contact information. Syntax: snmp-server system-contact <INFO>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server system-contact command?
The syntax of the snmp-server system-contact command is: snmp-server system-contact <INFO>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server system-location command do?
The snmp-server system-location command sets the SNMP location information. The no form of this command removes the SNMP location information. Syntax: snmp-server system-location <INFO>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server system-location command?
The syntax of the snmp-server system-location command is: snmp-server system-location <INFO>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server vrf command do?

The snmp-server vrf command configures a VRF on which the SNMP agent listens for incoming requests. By default, the SNMP agent does not listen on any VRF. 6100 only supports default VRF. The no form of this command stops the SNMP agent from listening for incoming requests on the specified VRF. Syntax: snmp-server vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server vrf command?
The syntax of the snmp-server vrf command is: snmp-server vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>] command do?

The snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>] command creates an SNMP v3 context on the specified VRF. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP context. Configures the SNMP v3 SNMP engine-id allowing an administrator to configure a unique SNMP engine-id for the switch. This engine-id is used by the NMS management tool to identify and distinguish multiple switches on the same network. The no form of this command restores the default engine-id, created by the switch using a combination of the enterprise OID value and the switch's MAC address. Configures the SNMP v3 security level. The security level determines which SMNPv3 users defined by the command snmpv3 user are able to connect. The no form of this command changes the security level as follows: . Syntax: snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>] command?
The syntax of the snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>] command is: snmpv3 context <NAME> vrf <VRF-NAME> [community <STRING>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure CoPP?

To configure CoPP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure the default CoPP policy, edit an existing policy, or create a policy with the command copp- 2. Add, edit, or remove classes in the policy with the command class 3. If the policy is not the active policy on the switch, apply it with the command apply copp-policy 4. Review the CoPP policy configuration settings with the command show copp-policy. Commands shown in the procedure include: policy..
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the apply copp-policy command do?

The apply copp-policy command applies a CoPP policy to the switch, replacing the policy that is in effect. There may be a brief interruption in traffic flow to the management processor while the switch implements the change. Enter the no apply copp-policy <NAME> command with the name of a CoPP policy to unapply a CoPP policy and apply the default CoPP policy. This will only take effect if the specified policy is actively applied. Since there must always be a CoPP policy applied, this command effectively attempts to replace the applied CoPP policy with the default CoPP policy. The default CoPP policy cannot be unapplied using this command. Syntax: apply copp-policy { <NAME> | default }.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the apply copp-policy command?
The syntax of the apply copp-policy command is: apply copp-policy { <NAME> | default }.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the class command?
The syntax of the class command is: class <CLASS> {drop | priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE>}.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear copp-policy statistics command do?
The clear copp-policy statistics command resets statistics for all CoPP classes to zero. Syntax: clear copp-policy statistics.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear copp-policy statistics command?
The syntax of the clear copp-policy statistics command is: clear copp-policy statistics.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> command do?

The default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> command configures the default class that is automatically defined for all CoPP policies. The default class cannot be removed, but its configuration can be changed. The default class is applied to traffic that does not match any other class defined for a policy. Syntax: default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> command?
The syntax of the default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> command is: default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show copp-policy factory-default command do?
The show copp-policy factory-default command display the configuration for the factory-default CoPP policy. Syntax: show copp-policy factory-default [commands].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show copp-policy factory-default command?
The syntax of the show copp-policy factory-default command is: show copp-policy factory-default [commands]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show copp-policy statistics command do?
The show copp-policy statistics command displays statistics for all classes, or a single class, in the active CoPP policy. Syntax: show copp-policy statistics [class <CLASS> | default-class].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show copp-policy statistics command?
The syntax of the show copp-policy statistics command is: show copp-policy statistics [class <CLASS> | default-class]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the portfilter <INTERFACE-LIST> command do?

The portfilter <INTERFACE-LIST> command configures the specified ports so they do not egress any packets that were received on the source port specified in interface context. Syntax: portfilter <INTERFACE-LIST>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the portfilter <INTERFACE-LIST> command?
The syntax of the portfilter <INTERFACE-LIST> command is: portfilter <INTERFACE-LIST>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show portfilter command do?
The show portfilter command displays filter settings for all interfaces or a specific interface. Syntax: show portfilter [<IFNAME>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show portfilter command?
The syntax of the show portfilter command is: show portfilter [<IFNAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Device discovery and configuration?

Device discovery and configuration: Device profiles NOTE: Device profiles rely on role configurations. For information on role configurations, see the Security Guide. Device profiles are used to dynamically assign port attributes based on the type of devices connected, without having to create a RADIUS infrastructure. You can map device profiles to device groups. A device group contains various match criteria, which can be obtained from multiple sources, such as LLDP, CDP, and local MAC match. Device profiles contain port attributes to be assigned to the port when a connected device matches a device group. Device profiles are supported on different scenarios. It can be applied on interfaces that are configured with security (802.1X or MAC authentication), or applied based on L2 port (LLDP, CDP), or applied on standalone ports with the block-until-profile-applied command enabled. All the methods are mutually exclusive of each other.

section: Configuring a device profile for LLDP

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure a device profile for LLDP?

To configure a device profile for LLDP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Create an LLDP group with the command port-access lldp-group 2. Define rules for adding devices to an LLDP group with the command match 3. Define rules for ignoring devices so that they are not added to an LLDP group with the command 4. Create a device profile with the command port-access device-profile 5. Add the LLDP group with the command associate lldp-group 6. Add a role to a device profile with the command associate role. Make sure that the role is already 7. Enable the device profile with the command enable.

task: Configuring a device profile for LLDP

section: Configuring a device profile for CDP

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure a device profile for CDP?

To configure a device profile for CDP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Create a CDP group with the command port-access cdp-group 2. Define rules for adding devices to a CDP group with the command match 3. Define rules for ignoring devices so that they are not added to a CDP group with the command ignore 4. Create a device profile with the command port-access device-profile 5. Add a CDP group to a device profile with the command associate cdp-group 6. Add a role to a device profile with the command associate role. Make sure that the role is already 7. Enable a device profile with the command enable.

task: Configuring a device profile for CDP

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authentication port-access allow-lldp-bpdu command do?

The aaa authentication port-access allow-lldp-bpdu command allows all packets related to the LLDP BPDU (Bridge Protocol Data Unit) on a secure port. The no form of this command blocks the LLDP BPDU on a secure port. On a nonsecure port, the command has no effect. 148 AOS-CX 10.06 Fundamentals Guide. Syntax: aaa authentication port-access allow-lldp-bpdu.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication port-access allow-lldp-bpdu command?
The syntax of the aaa authentication port-access allow-lldp-bpdu command is: aaa authentication port-access allow-lldp-bpdu.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the associate cdp-group <GROUP-NAME> command do?

The associate cdp-group <GROUP-NAME> command associates a CDP (Cisco Discovery Protocol) group with a device profile. A maximum of two CDP groups can be associated with a device profile. The no form of this command removes a CDP group from a device profile. Associates an LLDP group with a device profile. A maximum of two LLDP groups can be associated with a device profile The no form of this command removes an LLDP group from a device profile. Associates a MAC group with a device profile. A maximum of two MAC groups can be associated with a device profile. The no form of this command removes a MAC group from a device profile. Syntax: associate cdp-group <GROUP-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the associate cdp-group <GROUP-NAME> command?
The syntax of the associate cdp-group <GROUP-NAME> command is: associate cdp-group <GROUP-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the associate role <ROLE-NAME> command do?

The associate role <ROLE-NAME> command associates a role with a device profile. Only one role can be associated with a device profile. For information on how to configure a role, see the port access role information in the Security Guide. The no form of this command removes a role from a device profile. Disables a device profile. The no form of this command enables a device profile. Enables a device profile. The no form of this command disables a device profile. Syntax: associate role <ROLE-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the associate role <ROLE-NAME> command?
The syntax of the associate role <ROLE-NAME> command is: associate role <ROLE-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the mac-group <MAC-GROUP-NAME> command do?

The mac-group <MAC-GROUP-NAME> command creates a MAC group or modifies an existing MAC group. A MAC group is used to classify connected devices based on the MAC address details, such as mask or OUI. A maximum of 32 MAC groups can be configured on the switch. A maximum of 2 MAC groups can be associated with a device profile. Each group accepts 64 match or ignore commands. The no form of this command removes a MAC group. Syntax: mac-group <MAC-GROUP-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the mac-group <MAC-GROUP-NAME> command?
The syntax of the mac-group <MAC-GROUP-NAME> command is: mac-group <MAC-GROUP-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the port-access cdp-group <CDP-GROUP-NAME> command do?

The port-access cdp-group <CDP-GROUP-NAME> command creates a CDP (Cisco Discovery Protocol) group or modifies an existing CDP group. A CDP Group is used to classify connected devices based on the CDP packet details advertised by the device. A maximum of 32 CDP groups can be configured on the switch. Each group accepts 64 match/ignore commands. The no form of this command removes a CDP group. Syntax: port-access cdp-group <CDP-GROUP-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the port-access cdp-group <CDP-GROUP-NAME> command?
The syntax of the port-access cdp-group <CDP-GROUP-NAME> command is: port-access cdp-group <CDP-GROUP-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the port-access device-profile <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME> command do?

The port-access device-profile <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME> command creates a new device profile and switches to the config-device-profile context. A maximum of 32 device profiles can be created. The no form of this command removes a device profile. Configures the switch to block the port until a profile match occurs for a device. This configuration is required when no security feature is enabled on the port. You must enable this mode or security on the port for local MAC match feature to operate. You must not enable both features on the same port at the same time. 168 AOS-CX 10.06 Fundamentals Guide NOTE: You must not combine any other AAA configurations with the block-until-profile-applied mode. The no form of this command removes a rule for adding devices to a MAC group. Creates an LLDP group or modifies an existing LLDP group. Syntax: port-access device-profile <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the port-access device-profile <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME> command?
The syntax of the port-access device-profile <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME> command is: port-access device-profile <DEVICE-PROFILE-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear lldp neighbors command do?
The clear lldp neighbors command clears all LLDP neighbor details. Syntax: clear lldp neighbors.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear lldp neighbors command?
The syntax of the clear lldp neighbors command is: clear lldp neighbors.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear lldp statistics command do?
The clear lldp statistics command clears all LLDP neighbor statistics. Syntax: clear lldp statistics.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear lldp statistics command?
The syntax of the clear lldp statistics command is: clear lldp statistics.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lldp command do?

The lldp command enables LLDP support globally on all active interfaces. By default, LLDP is enabled. The no form of this command disables LLDP support globally on all active interfaces. It does not remove any LLDP configuration settings. Sets the 802.3 TLVs to be advertised. By default, advertisement of both POE and MAC/PHY TLVs is enabled. The no form of this command disables advertisement of 802.3 TLVs. Sets the holdtime that is used to calculate the LLDP Time-to-Live value. Time-to-Live defines the length of time that neighbors consider LLDP information sent by this agent as valid. When Time-to-Live expires, the information is deleted by the neighbor. Time-to-live is calculated by multiplying holdtime by the value of lldp timer. The no form of this command sets the holdtime to its default value of 4. Syntax: lldp.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the lldp command?
The syntax of the lldp command is: lldp.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lldp med-location {civic-addr | elin-addr } command do?

The lldp med-location {civic-addr | elin-addr } command configures support for the LLDP-MED TLV. Supports only civic address and emergency location information number (ELIN). Coordinate-based location is not supported. The no form of this command disables support for the LLDP MED TLV. Enables reception of LLDP information on an interface. By default, LLDP reception is enabled on all active interfaces. The no form of this command disables reception of LLDP information on an interface. Sets the amount of time (in seconds) to wait before performing LLDP initialization on an interface. The no form of this command sets the reinitialization time to its default value of 2 seconds. Syntax: lldp med-location {civic-addr | elin-addr }.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the lldp med-location {civic-addr | elin-addr } command?
The syntax of the lldp med-location {civic-addr | elin-addr } command is: lldp med-location {civic-addr | elin-addr }.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lldp select-tlv <TLV-NAME> command do?

The lldp select-tlv <TLV-NAME> command selects a TLV that the LLDP agent will send and receive. By default, all supported TLVs are sent and received. The no form of this command stops the LLDP agent from sending and receiving a specific TLV. Sets the interval (in seconds) at which local LLDP information is updated and TLVs are sent to neighboring network devices by the LLDP agent. The minimum setting for this timer must be four times the value of lldp txdelay. For example, this is a valid configuration: • lldp timer = 16 • lldp txdelay = 4 And, this is an invalid configuration: • lldp timer = 5 • lldp txdelay = 2 NOTE: When copying a saved configuration to the running configuration, the value for lldp timer is applied before the value of lldp txdelay. Syntax: lldp select-tlv <TLV-NAME>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the lldp select-tlv <TLV-NAME> command?
The syntax of the lldp select-tlv <TLV-NAME> command is: lldp select-tlv <TLV-NAME>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp local-device command do?

The show lldp local-device command shows global LLDP information advertised by the switch, as well as port-based data. If VLANs are configured on any active interfaces, the VLAN ID is only shown for trunk native or untagged VLAN IDs on access interfaces. Syntax: show lldp local-device.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp local-device command?
The syntax of the show lldp local-device command is: show lldp local-device.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp neighbor-info command do?

The show lldp neighbor-info command displays information about neighboring devices for all interfaces or for a specific interface. The information displayed varies depending on the type of neighbor connected and the type of TLVs sent by the neighbor. Syntax: show lldp neighbor-info [<INTERFACE-NAME>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp neighbor-info command?
The syntax of the show lldp neighbor-info command is: show lldp neighbor-info [<INTERFACE-NAME>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp neighbor-info detail command do?
The show lldp neighbor-info detail command shows detailed LLDP neighbor information for all LLDP neighbor connected interfaces. Syntax: show lldp neighbor-info detail.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp neighbor-info detail command?
The syntax of the show lldp neighbor-info detail command is: show lldp neighbor-info detail.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp statistics command do?
The show lldp statistics command shows global LLDP statistics or statistics for a specific interface. Syntax: show lldp statistics [<INTERFACE-ID>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp statistics command?
The syntax of the show lldp statistics command is: show lldp statistics [<INTERFACE-ID>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show lldp tlv command do?
The show lldp tlv command shows the LLDP TLVs that are configured for send and receive. Syntax: show lldp tlv.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show lldp tlv command?
The syntax of the show lldp tlv command is: show lldp tlv.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear cdp counters command do?
The clear cdp counters command clears CDP counters. Syntax: clear cdp counters.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear cdp counters command?
The syntax of the clear cdp counters command is: clear cdp counters.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear cdp neighbor-info command do?
The clear cdp neighbor-info command clears CDP neighbor information. Syntax: clear cdp neighbor-info.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear cdp neighbor-info command?
The syntax of the clear cdp neighbor-info command is: clear cdp neighbor-info.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show cdp command do?
The show cdp command shows CDP information for all interfaces. Syntax: show cdp.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show cdp command?
The syntax of the show cdp command is: show cdp.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show cdp neighbor-info command do?
The show cdp neighbor-info command shows CDP information for all neighbors or for CDP information on a specific interface. Syntax: show cdp neighbor-info <INTERFACE-ID>.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show cdp neighbor-info command?
The syntax of the show cdp neighbor-info command is: show cdp neighbor-info <INTERFACE-ID>
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Zero Touch Provisioning?

Zero Touch Provisioning: (ZTP) enables the auto-configuration of factory default switches without a network administrator onsite. When a switch is booted from its factory default configuration, ZTP autoprovisions the switch by automatically downloading and installing a firmware file, a configuration file, or both. With ZTP, even a nontechnical user (for example: a store manager in a retail chain or a teacher in a school) can deploy devices at a site. ZTP support The switch supports standards-based Zero Touch Provisioning (ZTP) operations as follows: • The switch must be running the factory default configuration. • ZTP operations are supported over IPv4 connections only. IPv6 connections are not supported for ZTP operations. • You must configure the DHCP server to provide a standards-based ZTP server solution. Options and features that are specific to Network Management Solution (NMS) tools, such as AirWave, are not supported.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ztp information command do?
The show ztp information command shows information about Zero Touch Provisioning (ZTP) operations performed on the switch. Syntax: show ztp information.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ztp information command?
The syntax of the show ztp information command is: show ztp information.

The clear events command clears up event logs. Using the show events command will only display the logs generated after the clear events command. Syntax: clear events.

For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show environment fan command do?

The show environment led command shows state and status information for all the configurable LEDs in the system. Syntax: show environment led.

For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show environment temperature command do?

The show hostname command shows the current host name. Syntax: show hostname.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show hostname command?
