For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show images command do?
The show images command shows information about the software in the primary and secondary images. Syntax: show images.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show images command?
The syntax of the show images command is: show images.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show running-config command do?
The show running-config command shows the current nondefault configuration running on the switch. No user information is displayed. Syntax: show running-config [<FEATURE>] [all].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show running-config command?
The syntax of the show running-config command is: show running-config [<FEATURE>] [all]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show running-config current-context command do?
The show running-config current-context command shows the current non-default configuration running on the switch in the current command context. Syntax: show running-config current-context.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show running-config current-context command?
The syntax of the show running-config current-context command is: show running-config current-context.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show startup-config command do?

The show startup-config command shows the contents of the startup configuration. NOTE: Switches in the factory-default configuration do not have a startup configuration to display. Syntax: show startup-config [json].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show startup-config command?
The syntax of the show startup-config command is: show startup-config [json]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show system command do?
The show system command shows general status information about the system. Syntax: show system.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show system command?
The syntax of the show system command is: show system.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show tech command?
The syntax of the show tech command is: show tech [basic | <FEATURE>] [local-file]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show usb command do?
The show usb command shows the USB port configuration and mount settings. Syntax: show usb.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show usb command?
The syntax of the show usb command is: show usb.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show usb file-system command do?

The show usb file-system command shows directory listings for a mounted USB device. When entered without the <PATH> parameter the top level directory tree is shown. Syntax: show usb file-system [<PATH>].
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show usb file-system command?
The syntax of the show usb file-system command is: show usb file-system [<PATH>]
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show version command do?

The show version command shows version information about the network operating system software, service operating system software, and BIOS. Configures the polling interval for system resource information collection and recording such as CPU and memory usage. Shows CPU utilization information. Shows memory utilization information. Syntax: show version.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show version command?
The syntax of the show version command is: show version.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the usb command do?

The usb command enables the USB ports on the switch. This setting is persistent across switch reboots and management module failovers. Both active and standby management modules are affected by this setting. The no form of this command disables the USB ports. Enables or disables the inserted USB drive. Syntax: usb.
For 6100 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the usb command?
The syntax of the usb command is: usb.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface loopback command do?
The interface loopback command creates a loopback interface and enters loopback configuration mode. The no form of this command deletes a loopback interface. Syntax: interface loopback <INSTANCE>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface loopback command?
The syntax of the interface loopback command is: interface loopback <INSTANCE>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip address command do?

The ip address command sets the IPv4 address for a loopback interface. The no form of this command reverses the set of the IPv4 address for a loopback interface. Syntax: ip address <IPv4-ADDR/MASK> [secondary].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip address command?
The syntax of the ip address command is: ip address <IPv4-ADDR/MASK> [secondary]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 address command do?
The IPv6 address command sets the IPv6 address for a loopback interface. 14. Syntax: IPv6 address <IPv6-ADDR/MASK>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 address command?
The syntax of the IPv6 address command is: IPv6 address <IPv6-ADDR/MASK>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface loopback command?
The syntax of the show interface loopback command is: show interface loopback [brief | instance <ID>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route command do?

The ip route command adds an IPv4 static route on the default VRF. The no form of this command deletes a IPv4 static route. Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route command?
The syntax of the ip route command is: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route distance command do?

The ip route distance command configures the administrative distance for the IPv4 static route. The no form of this command deletes the static route. Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>] distance <VALUE>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route distance command?
The syntax of the ip route distance command is: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>] distance <VALUE>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route tag command do?

The ip route tag command configures tag for IPv4 static route. The no form of this command deletes tag for IPv4 static route. Syntax: ip route <A. B. C. D/M> [A. B. C. D | INTERFACE] tag <1-4294967295>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route tag command?
The syntax of the ip route tag command is: ip route <A. B. C. D/M> [A. B. C. D | INTERFACE] tag <1-4294967295>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 route command do?

The IPv6 route command adds an IPv6 static route. The no form of this command deletes an IPv6 static route on the default VRF. Syntax: IPv6 route <DEST-IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 route command?
The syntax of the IPv6 route command is: IPv6 route <DEST-IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 route tag command do?
The IPv6 route tag command configures tag for IPv6 static route. Syntax: IPv6 route <X: X: : X: X/M> [X: X: : X: X | INTERFACE] tag <1-4294967295>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 route tag command?
The syntax of the IPv6 route tag command is: IPv6 route <X: X: : X: X/M> [X: X: : X: X | INTERFACE] tag <1-4294967295>

section: Configuring OSPF on the routing switch

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure OSPF on the routing switch?

To configure OSPF on the routing switch, follow the documented procedure: 1. Create the OSPF instance and enter the OSPF router configuration context using the following command 2. Configure a global router ID using the following command. For command details, see router-id. Commands shown in the procedure include: router ospf <PROCESS-ID>; router-id <ROUTER-ADDRESS>.

task: Configuring OSPF on the routing switch

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary] command do?

The area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary] command sets the cost of the default route announced to NSSA or stub areas. The no form of this command resets the cost of the default route announced to NSSA or stub areas, to the default value of 1. Creates the NSSA area (Not So Stubby Area) with <AREA-ID> if not present. If area is present and not NSSA area, this command changes the area type to NSSA area. If no-summary is used, area type will be NSSA No- Summary. The no form of this command unsets the area type as NSSA. That is, the configured area will be changed to default normal area. The no area <AREA-ID> nssa no-summary command enables sending inter-area routes into NSSA, but will not unset the area as NSSA. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary] command?
The syntax of the area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary] command is: area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear ip ospf statistics command do?
The clear ip ospf statistics command clear the OSPF event statistics. Syntax: clear ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear ip ospf statistics command?
The syntax of the clear ip ospf statistics command is: clear ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default-information originate command do?

The default-information originate command configures OSPF to advertise the default route (0.0.0.0/0) to its neighbors if it is present in the routing table. The no form of this command disables advertisement of the default route. Configures OSPF to advertise the default route (0.0.0.0/0) to its neighbors, regardless if it is present in the routing table or not. The no form of this command sets the OSPF administrative distance to the default of 110. Syntax: default-information originate.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the default-information originate command?
The syntax of the default-information originate command is: default-information originate.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default-metric <METRIC-VALUE> command do?

The default-metric <METRIC-VALUE> command sets the default metric for redistributed routes in the OSPF. The no form of this command sets the default metric to be used for redistributed routes into OSPF to the default of 25. Disables the OSPF process. IMPORTANT: This command does not remove the OSPF configurations. Syntax: default-metric <METRIC-VALUE>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the default-metric <METRIC-VALUE> command?
The syntax of the default-metric <METRIC-VALUE> command is: default-metric <METRIC-VALUE>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the distance <DISTANCE> command do?

The distance <DISTANCE> command defines an administrative distance for OSPF. Administrative distance is used as a criteria to select the best route when multiple routes are present from different routing protocols. The no form of this command sets the OSPF administrative distance to the default of 110. Enables the OSPF process, if disabled. By default the OSPF process is enabled. 46. Syntax: distance <DISTANCE>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the distance <DISTANCE> command?
The syntax of the distance <DISTANCE> command is: distance <DISTANCE>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper} command do?

The graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper} command configures graceful restart parameters for OSPF. The no form of this command sets the restart interval to the default interval of 120 seconds or disables the helper mode, depending on the parameter specified. Syntax: graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper} command?
The syntax of the graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper} command is: graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf area command do?

The ip ospf area command runs the OSPF protocol on the interface with the configured IPv4 address for the area specified. The interfaces which have an IP address configured in this network or in a subset of this network, will participate in the OSPF protocol. To move an interface to a new area, unmap the existing area and then associate a new area with the interface. The no form of this command disables OSPF on the interface and removes the interface from the area. Interfaces which have an IP address configured on the network or in a subset of the network, stop participating in the OSPF protocol. Syntax: ip ospf <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf area command?
The syntax of the ip ospf area command is: ip ospf <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf authentication command do?

The ip ospf authentication command sets the authentication type that will be used for authentication with the neighbor router. The no form of this command deletes the authentication type used for a particular authentication with the neighbor router and sets to null authentication. Syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}.

syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf authentication command?
The syntax of the ip ospf authentication command is: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}.

syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf authentication-key command do?
The ip ospf authentication-key command is documented in this guide. Syntax: ip ospf authentication-key {ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf authentication-key command?
The syntax of the ip ospf authentication-key command is: ip ospf authentication-key {ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf cost command do?

The ip ospf cost command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface. The interface cost is used as a parameter to calculate the best routes. The no form of this command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface to the default cost 1. Syntax: ip ospf cost <INTERFACE-COST>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf cost command?
The syntax of the ip ospf cost command is: ip ospf cost <INTERFACE-COST>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf dead-interval command do?

The ip ospf dead-interval command sets the interval after which a neighbor is declared dead if no hello packet is received on the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the interval after which a neighbor is declared dead, to the default for the OSPF interface. The default value is 40 seconds (generally 4 times the hello packet interval). Syntax: ip ospf dead-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf dead-interval command?
The syntax of the ip ospf dead-interval command is: ip ospf dead-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf hello-interval command do?

The ip ospf hello-interval command sets the time interval between OSPF hello packets for the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the time interval OSPF hello packets to the default of 10 seconds for the OSPF interface. Syntax: ip ospf hello-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf hello-interval command?
The syntax of the ip ospf hello-interval command is: ip ospf hello-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf keychain command do?

The ip ospf keychain command sets the key chain for md5 authentication. A key chain configures rotating keys for packet authenticating, reducing the risk of keys being compromised. The no form of this command deletes the key chain used for md5 authentication. Syntax: ip ospf keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf keychain command?
The syntax of the ip ospf keychain command is: ip ospf keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf network command do?

The ip ospf network command configures the OSPF network type for the interface. The no form of this command sets the network type for the interface to the system default which is broadcast network. Syntax: ip ospf network {broadcast | point-to-point}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf network command?
The syntax of the ip ospf network command is: ip ospf network {broadcast | point-to-point}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf passive command do?

The ip ospf passive command configures the interface as an OSPF passive interface. With this setting, the interface participates in OSPF but does not send or receive packets on that interface. The no form of this command resets the interface as active. With this setting, the interface starts sending and receiving OSPF packets. Syntax: ip ospf passive.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf passive command?
The syntax of the ip ospf passive command is: ip ospf passive.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf priority command do?

The ip ospf priority command sets the OSPF priority for the interface. The larger the numeric value of the priority, the higher the chances for it to become the designated router. Setting a priority of zero makes the router ineligible to become a designated router or back up designated router. The no form of this command sets the OSPF priority for the interface to the default of 1. Syntax: ip ospf priority <PRIORITY-VALUE>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf priority command?
The syntax of the ip ospf priority command is: ip ospf priority <PRIORITY-VALUE>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf retransmit-interval command do?

The ip ospf retransmit-interval command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements for the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements to the default of 5 seconds for the OSPF interface. Syntax: ip ospf retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf retransmit-interval command?
The syntax of the ip ospf retransmit-interval command is: ip ospf retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf shutdown command do?

The ip ospf shutdown command disables OSPF on the interface. The interface state changes to Down. It does not remove the interface from the OSPF area. To remove the interface, use the command no ip ospf area. The no form of this command re-enables OSPF on the interface. Syntax: ip ospf shutdown.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf shutdown command?
The syntax of the ip ospf shutdown command is: ip ospf shutdown.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf transit-delay command do?

The ip ospf transit-delay command sets the time delay in link state transmission for the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the delay in link state transmission to the default of 1 second for the OSPF interface. Syntax: ip ospf transit-delay <DELAY>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf transit-delay command?
The syntax of the ip ospf transit-delay command is: ip ospf transit-delay <DELAY>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME> command do?

The keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME> command sets the key chain for md5 authentication. A key chain configures rotating keys for packet authenticating, reducing the risk of keys being compromised. The no form of this command deletes the key chain used for md5 authentication. Syntax: keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME> command?
The syntax of the keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME> command is: keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]] command do?

The max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]] command sets the protocol to advertise a maximum metric so that other routers do not prefer this router as an intermediate hop in their shortest path first (SPF) calculations. If the on-startup parameter is used, the router is configured to advertise a maximum metric at startup for the time mentioned in seconds or for a default value of 600 seconds. The no form of this command advertises the normal cost metrics instead of advertising the maximized cost metric. This setting causes the router to be considered in traffic forwarding. Configures all OSPF interfaces as passive. The no form of this command sets all OSPF interfaces as active. Syntax: max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]] command?
The syntax of the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]] command is: max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>] command do?

The redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>] command redistributes routes originating from other protocols, or from another OSPF process, to the current OSPF process. The no form of this command disables redistribution of routes to the current OSPF process. Syntax: redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>] command?

The syntax of the redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>] command is: redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH> command do?

The reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH> command sets the reference bandwidth for OSPFv2. If the OSPFv2 interface cost is not explicitly set. then the cost of all the OSPFv2 interfaces is recalculated based on the reference bandwidth and link speed of the interface. For VLAN interfaces the link speed value is taken as 1 Gbps (if the OSPFv2 interface cost is not explicitly set). The no form of this command sets the reference bandwidth for OSPF to the default of 100000 Mbps. Syntax: reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH> command?
The syntax of the reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH> command is: reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router ospf command do?

The router ospf command creates an OSPF process (if not created already) on a VRF, and switches to the OSPF router instance context. Up to eight OSPF processes are supported per VRF. The no form of this command removes the OSPF instance. Syntax: router ospf <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the router ospf command?
The syntax of the router ospf command is: router ospf <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router-id command do?

The router-id command sets an ID for the router in an IPv4 address format. The no form of this command unconfigures the router-id for the instance and sets the router-id to the default as follows: the router-id is selected dynamically as equal to the highest loopback address on the router, or the highest active interface if there are no loopback addresses. If no IP address is configured on any interfaces on the router, OSPF will not form an adjacency. Syntax: router-id <ROUTER-ADDR>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the router-id command?
The syntax of the router-id command is: router-id <ROUTER-ADDR>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf command?
The syntax of the show ip ospf command is: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf interface command do?
The show ip ospf interface command displays general OSPF, area, state, and configuration information. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] interface [<interface-name>] [brief].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf interface command?
The syntax of the show ip ospf interface command is: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] interface [<interface-name>] [brief]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf lsdb command do?
The show ip ospf lsdb command is documented in this guide. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] lsdb [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf lsdb command?
The syntax of the show ip ospf lsdb command is: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] lsdb [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf routes command do?
The show ip ospf routes command is documented in this guide. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] routes.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf routes command?
The syntax of the show ip ospf routes command is: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] routes.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf statistics command do?
The show ip ospf statistics command displays OSPF statistics. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf statistics command?
The syntax of the show ip ospf statistics command is: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command do?

The summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command summarizes the external routes with the matching address and mask. When advertising this route, its metric is set to the lowest cost path from among the routes that were summarized. The no form of this command disables route summarization. NOTE: This command only works for an ASBR (Autonomous System Boundary Router). Syntax: summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command?
The syntax of the summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command is: summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the timers lsa-arrival <DELAY> command do?

The timers lsa-arrival <DELAY> command configures the minimum delay between receiving the same LSA from a peer. The same LSA is an LSA that contains the same LSA ID number, LSA type, and advertising router ID. If an instance of the same LSA arrives sooner before the delay expires, the LSA is dropped. Generally, the LSA arrival timer should be set to a value less than or equal to the start-time value for the command timers throttle lsa start on the neighbor. The no form of this command sets the LSA timers to default values. Syntax: timers lsa-arrival <DELAY>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the timers lsa-arrival <DELAY> command?
The syntax of the timers lsa-arrival <DELAY> command is: timers lsa-arrival <DELAY>

section: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME> command do?

The timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME> command configures the timers for LSA generation. The no form of this command sets the LSA timers to default values. Configures timers for SPF calculation. There are three timers: . Syntax: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>.

command: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>
syntax: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>

section: Configuring external route redistribution and control

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure external route redistribution and control?

To configure external route redistribution and control, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable route redistribution using the following command and specify one of the following parameters: 2. Optionally, modify the default metric for redistribution using the following command. For command 3. Optionally, set the cost of default-summary LSAs using the following command. For command details, .

task: Configuring external route redistribution and control

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> command do?

The area <AREA-ID> command creates a normal area with <AREA-ID> set if not present. If area is present and is not the normal area, this command changes the area type to normal area. The no form of this command deletes the area with the <AREA-ID> specified. The area can be of any type (stub, stub no-summary, and default normal area). Syntax: area <AREA-ID>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> command?
The syntax of the area <AREA-ID> command is: area <AREA-ID>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command do?

The area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command configures IPsec AH authentication for the specified area. OSPFv3 interfaces which have IPsec configured at the interface context will not use area level IPsec. The no form of this command removes IPsec AH authentication for the specified area. NOTE: IPsec is not supported for 6in6 tunnel interfaces. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command?

The syntax of the area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command is: area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command do?

The area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command configures IPsec ESP with authentication/encryption algorithm type and key for the specified area. OSPFv3 interfaces with IPsec configured at interface context will not use area level IPsec ESP configuration. The no form of this command removes IPsec ESP from the specified area. NOTE: IPsec is not supported for 6in6 tunnel interfaces. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command?
The syntax of the area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command is: area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command do?

The clear IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command clears the OSPFv3 event statistics. Sets the default metric for redistributed routes in the OSPFv3. Syntax: clear IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command?
The syntax of the clear IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command is: clear IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the disable command do?
The disable command disables the OSPFv3 process. By default OSPFv3 process is enabled. IMPORTANT: This command does not remove the OSPFv3 configurations. Syntax: disable.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the disable command?
The syntax of the disable command is: disable.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check} command do?

The graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check} command configures graceful restart for OSPFv3. By default graceful restart is enabled on the OSPFv3 router. The no form of this command sets the restart interval to the default of 120 seconds or disables helper mode depending on the specified parameters. Syntax: graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check} command?
The syntax of the graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check} command is: graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 area command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 area command runs the OSPFv3 protocol on the interface for the area specified. To move an interface to a new area, unmap the existing area and then associate a new area with the interface. The no form of this command disables OSPF on the interface and removes the interface from the area. Interfaces which have an IP address configured on the network or in a subset of the network, stop participating in the OSPF protocol. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 area command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 area command is: IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null command do?
The IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null command is documented in this guide. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null command is: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec command configures IPSec AH authentication. OSPFv3 interfaces that have IPsec configured at the interface context will not use area level IPsec. The no form of this command removes IPsec AH authentication for the specified area. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec command is: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 cost command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 cost command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface. The interface cost is used as a parameter to calculate the best routes. The no form of this command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface to the default of 1. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 cost <INTERFACE-COST>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 cost command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 cost command is: IPv6 ospfv3 cost <INTERFACE-COST>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval command do?
The IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval command is documented in this guide. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval command is: IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec command configures IPsec ESP. OSPFv3 interfaces that have IPsec configured at the interface context will not use area level IPsec ESP. The no form of this command removes IPsec ESP for the specified area. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec command is: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null command do?
The IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null command configures NULL ESP on an interface which disables IPsec ESP. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null command is: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval command sets the time interval between OSPFv3 hello packets for the OSPFv3 interface. The no form of this command sets the time interval between OSPFv3 hello packets to the default for the OSPFv3 interface of 10 seconds. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval command is: IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 network command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 network command configures the network type for the interface. By default the network type is broadcast network. The no form of this command sets the network type for the interface to the system default of broadcast network. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 network {broadcast|point-to-point}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 network command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 network command is: IPv6 ospfv3 network {broadcast|point-to-point}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 passive command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 passive command configures the interface as an OSPFv3 passive interface. With this setting, the interface participates in the OSPF, but does not send or receive OSPF packets on that interface. The no form of this command resets the interface as active. With this setting, the interface starts sending and receiving OSPF packets. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 passive.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 passive command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 passive command is: IPv6 ospfv3 passive.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 priority command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 priority command sets the OSPFv3 priority for the interface. The larger the numeric value of the priority, the higher the chance it will become the designated router. Setting a priority of 0 makes the router ineligible to become a designated router or back up designated router. The no form of this command sets the OSPFv3 priority for the interface to the default of 1. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 priority <number-value>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 priority command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 priority command is: IPv6 ospfv3 priority <number-value>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements for the OSPFv3 interface. The no form of this command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements to the default 5 seconds. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval command is: IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown command disables OSPFv3 on the interface. The interface state changes to Down. It does not remove the interface from the OSPF area. To remove the interface, use the command no ip ospf area. The no form of this command re-enables OSPFv3 on the interface. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown command is: IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay command sets the time delay in Link state transmission for the OSPFv3 interface. The no form of this command sets the transit delay in Link state transmission to the default of 1 second. Sets the maximum number of ECMP routes that OSPFv3 can support. The no form of this command sets the maximum number of ECMP routes that OSPFv3 can support to the default value of 4. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay <DELAY>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay command is: IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay <DELAY>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>] command do?

The max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>] command sets the protocol to advertise a maximum metric so that other routers do not prefer the router as an intermediate hop in their shortest path first (SPF) calculations. If the on-startup parameter is used, the router is configured to advertise a maximum metric at startup. That is, for the time specified in seconds, or the default value of 600 seconds. To disable advertisement of the maximum metric, use the no form of the command. The no form of this command advertises the normal cost metrics instead of advertising the maximized cost metric. This setting causes the router to be considered in traffic forwarding. Syntax: max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>] command?
The syntax of the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>] command is: max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the passive-interface default command do?

The passive-interface default command sets all OSPFv3 interfaces as passive. The no form of this command sets all the OSPFv3 interfaces as active. Redistributes routes originating from other protocols, or from another OSPF process, to the current OSPF process. The no form of this command disables redistribution of routes to the current OSPF process. Syntax: passive-interface default.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the passive-interface default command?
The syntax of the passive-interface default command is: passive-interface default.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the retransmit-interval <INTERVAL> command do?

The retransmit-interval <INTERVAL> command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements for virtual links. The no form of this command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements to the default of 5 seconds for virtual links. Syntax: retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the retransmit-interval <INTERVAL> command?
The syntax of the retransmit-interval <INTERVAL> command is: retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router ospfv3 command do?

The router ospfv3 command creates the OSPFv3 process (if not created already) and enters the router OSPFv3 instance context. Optionally if specified, you can specify a named VRF, or the default VRF if the <vrf-name> is not specified. Only one OSPFv3 process is allowed per VRF. 116. Syntax: router ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the router ospfv3 command?
The syntax of the router ospfv3 command is: router ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 command?
The syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 command is: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 border-routers command do?

The show IPv6 ospfv3 border-routers command shows the OSPFv3 routing table entries for Area Border Router (ABR) and Autonomous System Border Router (ASBR). Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] border-routers [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 border-routers command?
The syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 border-routers command is: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] border-routers [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 interface command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 interface command shows information about OSPFv3 enabled interfaces. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>] [brief].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 interface command?
The syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 interface command is: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>] [brief]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 neighbors command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 neighbors command shows information about OSPFv3 neighbors. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 neighbors command?
The syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 neighbors command is: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 routes command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 routes command shows the OSPFv3 routing table information. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] routes [<PREFIX/LENGTH>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 routes command?
The syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 routes command is: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] routes [<PREFIX/LENGTH>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command shows OSPFv3 statistics. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command?
The syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command is: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics interface command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics interface command shows the OSPFv3 statistics for the OSPFv3-enabled interfaces. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics interface command?
The syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics interface command is: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command do?

The summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command summarizes the external routes with the matching address and mask. When advertising this route, its metric is set to the lowest cost path from among the routes that were summarized. The no form of this command disables route summarization. NOTE: This command only works for an ASBR (Autonomous System Boundary Router). Syntax: summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command?
The syntax of the summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command is: summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME> command do?

The timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME> command configures timers for SPF calculation. There are three timers: • start-time Is the initial delay before an SPF calculation is started. Default is 200 milliseconds. • hold-time Is the progressive backoff time to wait before next scheduled SPF calculation. Default is 1000 milliseconds. If a route change event occurs during this period, the value doubles until it reaches the max-wait-time. • max-wait-time Is the maximum time to wait before the next scheduled SPF calculation. Default is 5000 milliseconds. This is used to limit the SPF hold timer and also defines the time to be considered for which the OSPF LSDB has to be stable, after which the SPF throttle mechanism is reset. The no form of this command sets all the configured non-default timers to default value. Syntax: timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME> command?
The syntax of the timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME> command is: timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the transit-delay <DELAY> command do?

The transit-delay <DELAY> command sets the time delay in Link state transmission for virtual links. The no form of this command sets the delay in Link state transmission to the default of 1 second for virtual links. Enables the notification of the events to be sent as traps to the SNMP management stations for OSPFv3. The no form of this command disables the notification of the events to be sent as traps to the SNMP management stations for OSPFv3. 130. Syntax: transit-delay <DELAY>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the transit-delay <DELAY> command?
The syntax of the transit-delay <DELAY> command is: transit-delay <DELAY>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the accept-lifetime [start-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>] {duration {<seconds> | infinite} | end-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>} command do?

The accept-lifetime [start-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>] {duration {<seconds> | infinite} | end-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>} command configures the duration for which the key is valid for receiving packets. The no form of this command configures the key packet receiving duration to the default value of an infinite time. Syntax: accept-lifetime [start-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>] {duration {<seconds> | infinite} | end-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the accept-lifetime [start-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>] {duration {<seconds> | infinite} | end-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>} command?

The syntax of the accept-lifetime [start-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>] {duration {<seconds> | infinite} | end-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>} command is: accept-lifetime [start-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>] {duration {<seconds> | infinite} | end-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the key <key-id> command do?

The key <key-id> command creates the key for a key chain and enters the key chain key context. A maximum of 64 keys can be configured per key chain. The no form of this command deletes the key from the key chain. Syntax: key <key-id>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the key <key-id> command?
The syntax of the key <key-id> command is: key <key-id>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the send-lifetime [start-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>] {duration {<seconds> | infinite} | end-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>} command do?

The send-lifetime [start-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>] {duration {<seconds> | infinite} | end-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>} command configures the duration for which the key is valid for sending packets. The no form of this command configures the key packet sending duration to the default value of an infinite time. Syntax: send-lifetime [start-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>] {duration {<seconds> | infinite} | end-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the send-lifetime [start-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>] {duration {<seconds> | infinite} | end-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>} command?

The syntax of the send-lifetime [start-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>] {duration {<seconds> | infinite} | end-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>} command is: send-lifetime [start-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>] {duration {<seconds> | infinite} | end-time <time> <month>/<day>/<year>}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show capacities keychain command do?
The show capacities keychain command shows the maximum number of key chains and keys configurable in a key chain. Syntax: show capacities keychain.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show capacities keychain command?
The syntax of the show capacities keychain command is: show capacities keychain.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show keychain command do?

The show keychain command shows information about configured and active keys of a named key chain or (if keychain-name is not specified) all configured key chains. Syntax: show keychain [<keychain-name>].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show keychain command?
The syntax of the show keychain command is: show keychain [<keychain-name>]

section: client track ip { enable | disable | auto }

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the client track ip { enable | disable | auto } command do?

The client track ip { enable | disable | auto } command enables client IP address tracking on the specified set of interfaces. Tracking will take effect only if client IP address tracking is enabled at both the system level and for the VLAN to which the port belongs. Default: auto. The no form of the command disables client IP address tracking on the specified set of interfaces. Configures the maximum number of clients to be tracked on the specified set of interfaces. For the 6100 Switch Series the no form of the command resets the client limit to the default value of 128. Syntax: client track ip { enable | disable | auto }.

command: client track ip { enable | disable | auto }
syntax: client track ip { enable | disable | auto }

section: client track ip { enable | disable | auto }

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the client track ip { enable | disable | auto } command?
The syntax of the client track ip { enable | disable | auto } command is: client track ip { enable | disable | auto }.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router ripng command do?

The router ripng command creates RIPng process if not already created and enters the router ripng <PROCESS-ID> context for the VRF mentioned. If no VRF is mentioned, a default is used. Only one RIPng process is allowed per VRF. The no form of this command deletes the RIPng instance for the VRF. If no VRF is mentioned the default is deleted. Syntax: router ripng <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show spanning-tree summary root command do?

The show spanning-tree summary root command shows spanning tree root summary information. Enables the spanning tree protocol on the switch. The no form of this command disables the spanning tree protocol on the switch. Enables the BDPU filter for the interface. The BPDU filter feature allows control of spanning tree participation on a per-port basis. It can be used to exclude specific ports from becoming part of spanning tree operations. A port with the BPDU filter enabled will ignore incoming BPDU packets and stay locked in the spanning tree forwarding state. All other ports maintain their role. Typical uses for this parameter include: • To have MSTP operations running on selected ports of the switch rather than every port of the switch at a time. • To prevent the spread of errant BPDU frames. Syntax: show spanning-tree summary root.

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear mvrp statistics command do?

The clear mvrp statistics command resets the MVRP statistic counters globally or for the specified ports or LAG. Enables the MVRP feature globally or on a specific interface. By default, MVRP is disabled. The no form of this command disables MVRP. Chapter 7 MVRP 141 NOTE: MVRP and VLAN translation cannot be enabled on the same interface. Configures the MVRP registrar state which determines how an MVRP participant responds to MRP messages. The default registration mode is normal. The no command removes the specified VLANs from the forbidden list. Sets an MVRP timer. The no form of this command sets the specified timer to its default value. Syntax: clear mvrp statistics [<PORT-NUM> | <PORT-LIST> | LAG <LAG-NUM>].

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is Multicast?

Multicast: Introduction to multicast Multicast addressing allows one-to-many or many-to-many communication among hosts on a network. Typical applications of multicast communication include: audio and video streaming, desktop conferencing, collaborative computing, and similar applications. IGMP snooping (Internet Group Management Protocol controls) can be configured per-VLAN basis to reduce unnecessary bandwidth usage. In the factory default state (IGMP and IGMP snooping disabled), the switch simply floods all IP multicast traffic it receives on a given VLAN through all ports on that VLAN (except the port on which it received the traffic). This can result in significant and unnecessary bandwidth usage in networks where IP multicast traffic is a factor. Enabling IGMP allows the ports to detect IGMP queries and report packets and manage IP multicast traffic through the switch. IGMP will be configured on the hosts, and multicast traffic will be generated by one or more servers (inside or outside of the local network).
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP)?

Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP): In a network where IP multicast traffic is transmitted for various multimedia applications, you can use the switch to reduce unnecessary bandwidth usage on a per-port basis by configuring IGMP (Internet Group Management Protocol). IGMPv3 (RFC 3376) and IGMPv2 (RFC 2236) are the current RFCs for IGMP. In the factory default state (IGMP disabled), the switch simply floods all IP multicast traffic it receives on a given VLAN through all ports on that VLAN (except the port on which it received the traffic). This can result in significant and unnecessary bandwidth usage in networks where IP multicast traffic is a factor. Enabling IGMP allows the ports to detect IGMP queries and report packets and manage IP multicast traffic through the switch. IGMP is useful in multimedia applications such as LAN TV, desktop conferencing, and collaborative computing, where there is Multi Point communication; that is, communication from one to many hosts, or communication originating from many hosts and destined for many other hosts.

The ip igmp command enables or disables IGMP on the current interface. IGMP is disabled by default. Syntax: ip igmp {enable | disable}.

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp apply access-list command do?

The ip igmp apply access-list command configures the ACL on a particular interface to filter the IGMP join or leave packets based on rules set in the particular ACL name. The no form of this command unconfigures the rules set for the ACL. NOTE: This configuration will override the ACL associated with IGMP snooping on the corresponding L2 VLAN. Syntax: ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME>.

The ip igmp querier interval command configures the interval between IGMP queries on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. The syntax of the ip igmp querier interval command is: ip igmp querier interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>

The ip igmp querier query-max-response-time command configures the IGMP querier max response time value in seconds on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. The no form of this command sets the querier max response time value to the default of 10 seconds on an interface. Syntax: ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME>.

The syntax of the ip igmp version strict command is: ip igmp version <VERSION> strict.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no ip igmp command do?
The no ip igmp command disables all IGMP configurations on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. Syntax: no ip igmp.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the no ip igmp command?
The syntax of the no ip igmp command is: no ip igmp.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp command do?
The show ip igmp command shows IGMP configuration information and status, or shows information by VRF. Syntax: show ip igmp [all-vrfs].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp command?
The syntax of the show ip igmp command is: show ip igmp [all-vrfs]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp counters command do?
The show ip igmp counters command shows IGMP counter details, or shows counters by VRF. Syntax: show ip igmp counters [all-vrfs].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp counters command?
The syntax of the show ip igmp counters command is: show ip igmp counters [all-vrfs]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp group command do?

The show ip igmp group command shows IGMP joined group information for the specified group, or shows joined group source and display information by VRF. Syntax: show ip igmp group <GROUP-IP> [source <SOURCE-IP>] [all-vrfs].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp group command?
The syntax of the show ip igmp group command is: show ip igmp group <GROUP-IP> [source <SOURCE-IP>] [all-vrfs]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp groups command do?
The show ip igmp groups command shows IGMP group information, or you can display group information by VRF. Syntax: show ip igmp groups [all-vrfs].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp groups command?
The syntax of the show ip igmp groups command is: show ip igmp groups [all-vrfs]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp interface {vlan <VLAN-ID>} command do?
The show ip igmp interface {vlan <VLAN-ID>} command shows IGMP configuration information for a specific interface (VLAN).
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp interface {vlan <VLAN-ID>} counters command do?
The show ip igmp interface {vlan <VLAN-ID>} counters command shows IGMP counter details for a specific interface or VLAN interface.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp interface {vlan <VLAN-ID>} group <GROUP-ID> [source <SOURCE-IP>] command do?
The show ip igmp interface {vlan <VLAN-ID>} group <GROUP-ID> [source <SOURCE-IP>] command shows IGMP joined group information for a specific interface or VLAN interface, or specify a source IP.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp interface {vlan <VLAN-ID>} groups command do?
The show ip igmp interface {vlan <VLAN-ID>} groups command shows IGMP group information for a specific interface or VLAN interface.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp interface {vlan <VLAN-ID>} statistics command do?
The show ip igmp interface {vlan <VLAN-ID>} statistics command shows IGMP statistics for a specific interface or VLAN interface, including groups joined.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp static-groups command do?
The show ip igmp static-groups command shows IGMP static groups, or shows information by VRF. Syntax: show ip igmp static-groups [all-vrfs].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp static-groups command?
The syntax of the show ip igmp static-groups command is: show ip igmp static-groups [all-vrfs]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp statistics command do?
The show ip igmp statistics command shows IGMP statistics, including groups joined, or shows statistics by VRF. Syntax: show ip igmp statistics [all-vrfs].
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp statistics command?
The syntax of the show ip igmp statistics command is: show ip igmp statistics [all-vrfs]
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping {enable disable} command do?

The ip igmp snooping {enable disable} command enables or disables IGMP snooping on the VLAN. By default, IGMP snooping is disabled. 42 AOS-CX 10.06 Multicast Guide. Syntax: ip igmp snooping {enable | disable}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping {enable disable} command?
The syntax of the ip igmp snooping {enable disable} command is: ip igmp snooping {enable | disable}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping apply access-list command do?

The ip igmp snooping apply access-list command configures the ACL on a particular interface to filter the IGMP join or leave packets based on rules set in the particular ACL name. The no form of this command unconfigures the rules set for the ACL. NOTE: This configuration will override the ACL associated with IGMP snooping on the corresponding L2 VLAN. Syntax: ip igmp snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping apply access-list command?
The syntax of the ip igmp snooping apply access-list command is: ip igmp snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping auto command do?

The ip igmp snooping auto command configures the specified ports in auto mode. In auto mode traffic flow is controlled by the IGMP joins/leaves. Auto mode is the default. The no form of this command removes auto mode ports for the VLAN. Syntax: ip igmp snooping auto <PORT-LIST>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping auto command?
The syntax of the ip igmp snooping auto command is: ip igmp snooping auto <PORT-LIST>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping blocked command do?

The ip igmp snooping blocked command configures the specified ports in blocked mode. In blocked mode, joins and traffic are always blocked on this port. The no form of this command disables blocked ports. Syntax: ip igmp snooping blocked <PORT-LIST>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping blocked command?
The syntax of the ip igmp snooping blocked command is: ip igmp snooping blocked <PORT-LIST>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping drop-unknown command do?

The ip igmp snooping drop-unknown command configures drop-unknown mode. While IGMP snooping is enabled, the traffic will be forwarded only to ports that made an IGMP request for the multicast. Drop unknown filters ensure that packets are not forwarded to ports that did not make a request for the traffic stream. This could either be a filter across all VLANs (vlan-shared) or per VLAN (vlan-exclusive). The default is vlan-shared. The no form of this command disables drop unknown on the switch. Syntax: ip igmp snooping drop-unknown {vlan-shared | vlan-exclusive}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping drop-unknown command?
The syntax of the ip igmp snooping drop-unknown command is: ip igmp snooping drop-unknown {vlan-shared | vlan-exclusive}.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping fastlearn command do?

The ip igmp snooping fastlearn command enables the port to learn group information when receiving a topology change notification. By default, fast learn is not enabled on ports. The no form of this command disables fast learn on the specified ports. Syntax: ip igmp snooping fastlearn <PORT-LIST>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping fastlearn command?
The syntax of the ip igmp snooping fastlearn command is: ip igmp snooping fastlearn <PORT-LIST>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping fastleave command do?

The ip igmp snooping fastleave command enables the switch to immediately remove the IGMP client from its IGMP table and cease transmitting multicast traffic to the client. The no form of this command disables fastleave on the specified ports. Syntax: ip igmp snooping fastleave <PORT-LIST>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping fastleave command?
The syntax of the ip igmp snooping fastleave command is: ip igmp snooping fastleave <PORT-LIST>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping forward command do?

The ip igmp snooping forward command configures the specified ports in forward mode. In forward mode, traffic is always forwarded on this port, irrespective of joins. 48 AOS-CX 10.06 Multicast Guide The no form of this command disables forward ports. Syntax: ip igmp snooping forward <PORT-LIST>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping forward command?
The syntax of the ip igmp snooping forward command is: ip igmp snooping forward <PORT-LIST>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping static-group command do?

The ip igmp snooping static-group command configures an IGMP snooping static multicast group. You can configure a maximum of 32 IGMP snooping static groups. The no form of this command disables static multicast group. Syntax: ip igmp snooping static-group <MULTICAST-IP-ADDRESS>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping static-group command?
The syntax of the ip igmp snooping static-group command is: ip igmp snooping static-group <MULTICAST-IP-ADDRESS>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping version command do?
The ip igmp snooping version command configures the IGMP snooping version on the VLAN. Syntax: ip igmp snooping version <VERSION>.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping version command?
The syntax of the ip igmp snooping version command is: ip igmp snooping version <VERSION>
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no ip igmp snooping command do?
The no ip igmp snooping command disables all IGMP snooping configurations on the VLAN. Syntax: no ip igmp snooping.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the no ip igmp snooping command?
The syntax of the no ip igmp snooping command is: no ip igmp snooping.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp snooping command do?
The show ip igmp snooping command shows IGMP snooping configuration information and status for all VLANs. Syntax: show ip igmp snooping.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp snooping command?
The syntax of the show ip igmp snooping command is: show ip igmp snooping.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp snooping counters command do?
The show ip igmp snooping counters command shows IGMP snooping query packet Tx, Rx, and Error packet counter details. Syntax: show ip igmp snooping counters.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp snooping counters command?
The syntax of the show ip igmp snooping counters command is: show ip igmp snooping counters.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp snooping groups command do?
The show ip igmp snooping groups command shows IGMP snooping group information. Syntax: show ip igmp snooping groups.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp snooping groups command?
The syntax of the show ip igmp snooping groups command is: show ip igmp snooping groups.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp snooping static-groups command do?
The show ip igmp snooping static-groups command shows IGMP snooping static group details. Syntax: show ip igmp snooping static-groups.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp snooping static-groups command?
The syntax of the show ip igmp snooping static-groups command is: show ip igmp snooping static-groups.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp snooping statistics command do?
The show ip igmp snooping statistics command shows IGMP snooping protocol information and the joined group statistics. Syntax: show ip igmp snooping statistics.
For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip igmp snooping statistics command?
The syntax of the show ip igmp snooping statistics command is: show ip igmp snooping statistics.

The show ip igmp snooping vlan <VLAN-ID> group port command shows IGMP snooping group details for the specified VLAN. It shows information about all IGMP snooping groups or sources learned on a particular port.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip igmp snooping vlan <VLAN-ID> statistics command do?
The show ip igmp snooping vlan <VLAN-ID> statistics command shows IGMP snooping statistics details for the specified VLAN. It also shows information on the different groups joined in the VLAN.

messages.content: For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 mld snooping command do?

The IPv6 mld snooping command this command enables or disables MLD snooping on the VLAN. The no form of this command disables all MLD snooping configurations on the VLAN. Syntax: IPv6 mld snooping {enable | disable}.

The IPv6 mld snooping fastlearn command this command enables the port to learn group information on receiving topology change notification. The no form of this command disables fastlearn on the ports. 60 AOS-CX 10.06 Multicast Guide. Syntax: IPv6 mld snooping fastlearn <port-list>.

The IPv6 mld snooping fastleave command enables the switch to immediately remove an interface from the bridge table upon receiving the leave group message. The no form of this command disables fastleave configuration on the ports. Syntax: IPv6 mld snooping fastleave <port-list>.

The IPv6 mld snooping forced fastleave command configures the given ports in forced fastleave mode. The no form of this command disables forced fastleave configuration on the ports. Syntax: IPv6 mld snooping forced fastleave <port-list>.

The no form of this command disables the rules set for the ACL. NOTE: This configuration will override the ACL associated with IGMP snooping on the corresponding L2 VLAN. Syntax: IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME>.

The syntax of the IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list command is: IPv6 mld snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME>

The IPv6 mld snooping [auto <port-list>] command this command configures the given ports in auto mode, which is the default port mode. Syntax: IPv6 mld snooping [auto <port-list>].

The IPv6 mld snooping [blocked <port-list>] command by default ports are configured in auto mode. This command configures the given ports in blocked mode. The no form of this command removes blocked ports. Syntax: IPv6 mld snooping [blocked <port-list>].

The IPv6 mld snooping [forward <port-list>] command by default ports are configured in auto mode. This command configures the given ports in forward mode. The no form of this command disables forward ports. Syntax: IPv6 mld snooping [forward <port-list>].

For 6200 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 mld robustness command do?

The IPv6 mld robustness command this command configures MLD robustness. The robustness value represents the number of times the querier retries queries on the connected subnets. The default robustness-value is 2 seconds. Syntax: IPv6 mld robustness <value>.
