For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Feature description?

Feature description: Virtual Switching Framework, or VSF, defines a virtual switch, comprising multiple individual physical switches, interconnected through standard Ethernet links. These physical switches will operate with one AOS-CX Virtual Switching Framework (VSF) Guide for 6200, 6300 Switch Series | User Guide 9 control plane, visible to peers as a virtual switch stack. This composition simplifies management and provides the capability to scale the stack. On-demand scalability in the access layer allows the user to increase the number of ports on a stack as per needs, without having to manage a new switch. The same stack can scale up or down to match the requirements.
- 6200F: VSF allows stacks to be formed using any combination of SKUs of the 6200 family. Up to 8 member switches will be allowed. Connections between the switches must use 10G links.
- 6300: VSF allows stacks to be formed using any combination of SKUs of the 6300 family. Up to 10 member switches will be allowed. Connections between the switches must use 10G, 25G, or 50G links.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is Feature?

Feature: VSF is always enabled on supported switches. Within the stack, one switch is the Master that runs all control plane software and manages the ASICs of all stack members. Any switch apart from primary can be configured as Standby switch. VSF behavior Each stack member must have a unique member number. When deploying a stack, ensure that each member has a distinct number by renumbering the switches to the appropriate member numbers. Stack formation will fail if there is a member number conflict.
- The primary member will become Master and the secondary member will become Standby under normal circumstances.
- The primary member is member number 1. This setting is not configurable and 1 is the default. A factory-default switch boots up as a VSF-enabled switch with a member number of 1.
- The secondary member number is user configurable, and there is no default secondary member. It is recommended that the customer configures a secondary member in the stack, since a stack with a standby offers resiliency and high-availability.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Stack management?

Stack management: Consoles The serial console of the Master switch provides a full CLI configuration interface for a user with valid credentials. The serial console of the other stack members, including the Standby, provides a reduced CLI configuration interface, with only a limited set of commands for troubleshooting the stack. In a standard deployment, connect to the console interface of the master and standby switch. This enables the stack master console to be reachable after a stack failover to the new Master. Any switch configuration or monitoring must be performed from the console of the stack Master switch only.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Management interface?

Management interface: In a VSF stack, only the management interface on the Master switch will be assigned an IP address (configured or assigned by DHCP). The stack allows connectivity to management protocols and Console through the management interface on the Master. VSF configuration The following aspects of VSF are user-configurable. Member number To add a device to a VSF stack, the device must be renumbered to the corresponding member ID. The user can specify the member number of the switch. The default member number is 1.
- For the 6200F device, the default number can be changed to any value from 2 through 8. (The device supports up to 8 members.) - For 6300 devices, the default number can be changed to any value from 2 through 10. (The device supports up to 10 members.) Refer to vsf renumber-to and Misconfiguration recovery for information about renumbering a member. Changing the member number causes the switch to reboot and all configuration on the switch is removed.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure a VSF stack?

To configure a VSF stack, follow the documented procedure: 1. Press Ctrl+C on the switch console 2. Log in using administrator credentials 3. At the prompt, issue the vsf-factory-reset command 4. This resets the member to factory-default settings and the switch will come up with a default 5. Now the user can reconfigure the VSF link and renumber it to the preferred member ID.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Failure and recovery?

Failure and recovery: The following section describes failure and recovery scenarios for stack split issues. Stack split Multiple link or member failures can result in a complete stack split, with the master and standby switch in different split fragments. In this case, both master and standby switches will become master switches. Each stack fragment will continue operating with the same configuration and state, and forward traffic between all stations that the fragment can reach. The downside of this scenario is that each stack fragment will have the same MAC address and IP addresses. To avoid this scenario, configure split detection (described in the following section) which would bring down the interfaces on one fragment to prevent duplicate MAC/IP s.
For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Management interface split detection?

Management interface split detection: For more information, refer to vsf split-detect. VSF stack supports management split detection, which requires users to connect the management interfaces of the primary and secondary stack members to the same L2 network. The Primary stack member is member "1", whereas the secondary member is the user-configured secondary switch. Once the stack is split, both of these switches become master of their respective fragments. The fragment with "1" as master is referred to as the primary fragment, and the fragment with the secondary switch as master is the secondary fragment. It is also possible to connect the management interfaces of primary and secondary to one another for split detection. If the secondary fragment discovers that the primary fragment is operational, it will bring down all front- plane non-VSF interfaces on the secondary fragment to minimize network disruption due to duplicate MAC or IP addresses. The interfaces will remain down until the stack is reconnected or the primary fragment goes down. The interfaces of the primary fragment will always remain operational.

For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Frequently asked questions?

Frequently asked questions: What is VSF? Virtual Switching Framework, or VSF, defines a single virtual switch comprised of multiple individual physical switches that are interconnected through standard Ethernet links. These links are referred to as VSF links. These physical switches will function as one device with a unified control and management plane. Multiport VSF links are supported. What are the supported platforms for VSF? The Aruba 6300F/M Switch Series supports VSF. VSF can be formed with a combination of any of the Aruba 6300F/M Switch Series (JL658A, JL659A, JL660A, JL661A, JL662A, JL663A, JL664A, JL665A, JL666A, JL667A, JL668A) or a combination of any of the Aruba 6200F Switch Series (JL724A, JL725A, JL726A, JL727A, JL728A). Aruba 6200F Switch Series only supports fixed SKUs. What port speeds do VSF links support? For Aruba 6300F/M Switch Series: All uplink ports with 10G, 25G, and 50G speeds can be configured as VSF links. For Aruba 6200F Switch Series: All uplink ports with 10G speed can be configured as VSF links. Aruba recommends that all VSF links be configured to run at the same speed.

For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the crossconnect input <in-label> output <IFNAME> command do?

The crossconnect input <in-label> output <IFNAME> command configures a static LSP binding to swap labels and route to the given next hop. The no form of this command removes the static LSP binding label swap configuration. Specifies the MPLS label to bind. Range: 16-1048575. <in-label> <IFNAME> <IP-ADDR> <out-label> Specifies the egress interface of the binding. Specifies the next hop IP address of the binding. Specifies the MPLS label to apply. Range: 16-1048575. explicit-null Specifies an IETF MPLS IPv4 explicit null label (0). Syntax: crossconnect input <in-label> output <IFNAME>.

For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the graceful-restart command do?

The graceful-restart command enables LDP graceful restart. Graceful restart is enabled by default. With graceful restart enabled, the MPLS forwarding state will be temporarily retained if the control plane restarts. The switch will wait after losing LDP neighbors before deleting bindings from that neighbor. See mpls ldp graceful restart timers for details. Graceful restart is enabled for LDP sessions only when the LDP setting and the overall router setting are enabled. If either is disabled, then graceful restart will not occur for LDP sessions. Upon being disabled or enabled, any LDP sessions will be restarted, which may result in temporary traffic loss. The no form of this command disables LDP graceful restart. Syntax: graceful-restart.

section: 3. Is VSX required on PEs for high availability?

messages.content: For 6300_6400 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about 3. Is VSX required on PEs for high availability? ?

3. Is VSX required on PEs for high availability? : VSX is not required on PEs as L3 routing across 2 standalone PEs (from a dual homed CE) provides PE redundancy. 4. How does the L3VPN solution enable site redundancy? Site redundancy can be achieved using dual standalone PE devices connected to the site CE device that requires redundancy instead of using the PE devices in VSX mode. In order to ensure optimal best path selection, BGP configurations should implemented as explained in the examples above with same RD configurations on both PE devices. Public Frequently asked questions 239 5. Are non-L3VPN forwarding deployments supported? Non-L3VPN forwarding deployments are supported with static LSP. Please refer to the l sp label imposition command for configuration details. LDP/BGP need to be configured on devices as explained in the examples above for all L3VPN deployments. 6.

topic: 3. Is VSX required on PEs for high availability?

For 8360 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Management of rotated log files?

Management of rotated log files: Rotated log files are compressed and stored locally in /var/log/, regardless of the remote host configuration. Rotated log files are stored with respective time extension to the granularity of hour in the format file1-YYYYMMDDHH.gz (for example, messages-2015080715.gz). Rotated log files are replaced when the number of old rotated log files exceeds three. The newly rotated log file replaces the oldest rotated log file. Remote transfer of rotated log files Only the TFTP protocol is supported for remote transfer, and both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses are supported. Only newly rotated log files are transferred to the remote host during the log rotation. Previously rotated log files are not transferred. After a file is successfully transferred, it is removed from the switch local path.
For 8360 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the logrotate maxsize <MAX-SIZE> command do?

The logrotate maxsize <MAX-SIZE> command specifies the maximum allowed log file size. The no form of this command resets the size of the log file to the default (100 MB). Sets the rotate period for the event logs, stored in the /var/log/event.log file, and authentication logs, stored in the /var/log/auth.log file. Defaults to daily. A log file that exceeds either the logrotate <MAX-SIZE> value or the logrotate period (whichever happens first), triggers rotation for that log file. The no form of this command resets the log rotation period to the default. Syntax: logrotate maxsize <MAX-SIZE>.
For 8360 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the logrotate maxsize <MAX-SIZE> command?
The syntax of the logrotate maxsize <MAX-SIZE> command is: logrotate maxsize <MAX-SIZE>

For 8360 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you troubleshoot?

To troubleshoot, follow the documented procedure: 1. Modify the ACL to allow the ping traffic 2. Unapply the ACL from egress (8400/8320/8325 switches) or Control Plane 3. Ping a destination which is not matched by the ACL. For example, if the ACL is blocking traffic based on. Commands shown in the procedure include: PING 100.1.2.10 (100.1.2.10) 100(128) bytes of data; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted.

section: Troubleshooting Web UI and REST API access issues

messages.content: For 8360 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you troubleshoot Web UI and REST API access issues?

To troubleshoot Web UI and REST API access issues, follow the documented procedure: 1. Log out from one of the existing sessions 2. If the session cookie is lost and it is not possible to log out of the session, then wait for the session idle.

task: Troubleshooting Web UI and REST API access issues

messages.content: For 8360 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure CoPP?

To configure CoPP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure the default CoPP policy, edit an existing policy, or create a policy with the command copp- 2. Add, edit, or remove classes in the policy with the command class 3. If the policy is not the active policy on the switch, apply it with the command apply copp-policy 4. Review the CoPP policy configuration settings with the command show copp-policy. Commands shown in the procedure include: policy..
For 8360 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the apply copp-policy command do?

The apply copp-policy command applies a CoPP policy to the switch, replacing the policy that is in effect. There may be a brief interruption in traffic flow to the management processor while the switch implements the change. Enter the no apply copp-policy <NAME> command with the name of a CoPP policy to unapply a CoPP policy and apply the default CoPP policy. This will only take effect if the specified policy is actively applied. Since there must always be a CoPP policy applied, this command effectively attempts to replace the applied CoPP policy with the default CoPP policy. The default CoPP policy cannot be unapplied using this command. Syntax: apply copp-policy { <NAME> | default }.
For 8360 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the apply copp-policy command?
The syntax of the apply copp-policy command is: apply copp-policy { <NAME> | default }.
For 8360 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the class command?
The syntax of the class command is: class <CLASS> {drop | priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>]}.
For 8360 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear copp-policy statistics command do?
The clear copp-policy statistics command resets statistics for all CoPP classes to zero. Syntax: clear copp-policy statistics.
For 8360 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear copp-policy statistics command?
The syntax of the clear copp-policy statistics command is: clear copp-policy statistics.
For 8360 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>] command do?

The default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>] command configures the default class that is automatically defined for all CoPP policies. The default class cannot be removed, but its configuration can be changed. The default class is applied to traffic that does not match any other class defined for a policy. Resets an active CoPP policy to match the settings that are currently in effect for the active policy on the switch. Changes made to the active policy that could not be activated are removed from the active policy. 14 AOS-CX 10.06 CoPP Guide When the switch fails to add or modify a class in an active CoPP policy, it is possible the active policy settings on the switch may be out of sync with those defined in the policy. Syntax: default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>].
For 8360 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>] command?
The syntax of the default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>] command is: default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>]

For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the redundancy switchover command do?

The redundancy switchover command causes the switch to immediately switch over to the Standby Management Module. This command must be executed from the Active Management Module and will fail if the Standby Management Module is in a failed state or not present. Syntax: redundancy switchover.

For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is BFD?

BFD: NOTE: The BFD feature and thus this entire chapter is not applicable to the 6200 Switch Series. Bidirectional Forwarding Detection (BFD) provides a general-purpose, standard, medium- and protocol- independent fast failure detection mechanism. It can detect and monitor the connectivity of links in IP to detect communication failures quickly. BFD operates independently of media, data protocols, and routing protocols. BFD establishes a session between two network devices to detect failures on the bidirectional forwarding paths between the devices and provide services for upper-layer protocols. BFD provides no neighbor discovery mechanism. Protocols that BFD services notify BFD of devices to which it needs to establish sessions. After a session is established, if no BFD control packet is received from the peer within the negotiated BFD interval, BFD notifies a failure to the protocol, which then takes appropriate measures. BFD operates in two modes: • Asynchronous mode: In this mode, an operating device periodically sends BFD control packets to another device.

section: Configuring BFD for an IPv4 static route

messages.content: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure BFD for an IPv4 static route?
To configure BFD for an IPv4 static route, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD support with the command bfd 2. Enable BFD on an IPv4 static route with the command ip route bfd.

task: Configuring BFD for an IPv4 static route

messages.content: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure BFD For OSPFv2?

To configure BFD For OSPFv2, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD support with the command bfd 2. Enable BFD on all OSPF interfaces with the command bfd all-interfaces, or enable BFD on a specific. Commands shown in the procedure include: interface with the command ip ospf bfd..
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure BFD For OSPFv3?

To configure BFD For OSPFv3, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD support with the command bfd 2. Enable BFD on all OSPF interfaces with the command bfd all-interfaces, or enable BFD on a specific. Commands shown in the procedure include: interface with the command IPv6 ospfv3 bfd..
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure BFD for VRRP?
To configure BFD for VRRP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD support with the command bfd 2. Enable BFD on a VRRP interface with the command bfd <IPv4-ADDR>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd command do?
The bfd command is documented in this guide. Syntax: bfd.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd command?
The syntax of the bfd command is: bfd.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command do?

The bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command enables BFD under VRRP for the specified IP address. BFD is asynchronous and echo mode is supported. The no form of this command disables BFD under VRRP for the specified IP address. Syntax: bfd <IPv4-ADDR>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command?
The syntax of the bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command is: bfd <IPv4-ADDR>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd all-interfaces command do?

The bfd all-interfaces command enables BFD on all OSPFv2 or OSPFv3 interfaces. The no form of this command disables BFD on all OSPFv2/OSPFv3 or PIM IPv4/IPv6 interfaces, excluding those on which BFD was enabled at the interface level with the commands ip ospf bfd and IPv6 ospfv3 bfd. Syntax: bfd all-interfaces.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd all-interfaces command?
The syntax of the bfd all-interfaces command is: bfd all-interfaces.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd detect-multiplier command do?
The bfd detect-multiplier command is documented in this guide. Syntax: bfd detect-multiplier <MULTIPLIER>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd detect-multiplier command?
The syntax of the bfd detect-multiplier command is: bfd detect-multiplier <MULTIPLIER>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd disable command do?
The bfd disable command disables BFD on the switch, but retains all configuration settings. Syntax: bfd disable.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd disable command?
The syntax of the bfd disable command is: bfd disable.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd enable command do?
The bfd enable command enables or disables BFD for HSC feature. Syntax: bfd enable.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd enable command?
The syntax of the bfd enable command is: bfd enable.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd echo disable command do?
The bfd echo disable command is documented in this guide. Syntax: bfd echo disable.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd echo disable command?
The syntax of the bfd echo disable command is: bfd echo disable.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd echo-src-ip-address command do?

The bfd echo-src-ip-address command sets the source IPv4 address for BFD echo packets. This address is used in all echo sessions. NOTE: The source IP address must not be on the same network segment as any switch interface, otherwise a large number of ICMP redirect packets may be sent by the remote device, causing network congestion. The no form of this command removes the source IPv4 address for BFD echo packets, which causes the switch to stop sending echo packets. When a valid value is set, all sessions with a peer that is capable of receiving echo packets, will start transmitting echo packets. BFD control sessions continue to run concurrently with echo packets. 30 AOS-CX 10.06 High Availability Guide. Syntax: bfd echo-src-ip-address <IPv4-ADDR>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd echo-src-ip-address command?
The syntax of the bfd echo-src-ip-address command is: bfd echo-src-ip-address <IPv4-ADDR>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd min-echo-receive-interval command do?

The bfd min-echo-receive-interval command sets the minimum time interval between received BFD echo packets. The no form of this command sets the minimum interval between received BFD echo packets to the default value of 500 milliseconds. Syntax: bfd min-echo-receive-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd min-echo-receive-interval command?
The syntax of the bfd min-echo-receive-interval command is: bfd min-echo-receive-interval <INTERVAL>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd min-receive-interval command do?

The bfd min-receive-interval command sets the minimum time interval between received BFD control packets on an interface. The no form of this command sets the minimum interval between received BFD control packets to the default value of 3000 milliseconds. Syntax: bfd min-receive-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd min-receive-interval command?
The syntax of the bfd min-receive-interval command is: bfd min-receive-interval <INTERVAL>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd min-transmit-interval command do?

The bfd min-transmit-interval command sets the minimum time interval between transmitted BFD control packets on an interface. The no form of this command sets the minimum interval between transmitted BFD control packets to the default value of 3000 ms. Syntax: bfd min-transmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd min-transmit-interval command?
The syntax of the bfd min-transmit-interval command is: bfd min-transmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear bfd statistics command do?
The clear bfd statistics command clears statistics for all BFD sessions or for a specific BFD session. Syntax: clear bfd statistics [session <ID>].
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear bfd statistics command?
The syntax of the clear bfd statistics command is: clear bfd statistics [session <ID>]
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf bfd command do?

The ip ospf bfd command enables BFD for OSPFv2 on the current interface. The interface must have OSPFv2 enabled on it. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. The no form of this command sets the current interface to the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Syntax: ip ospf bfd.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf bfd command?
The syntax of the ip ospf bfd command is: ip ospf bfd.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf bfd disable command do?
The ip ospf bfd disable command disables BFD for OSPFv2 on the current interface. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Syntax: ip ospf bfd disable.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf bfd disable command?
The syntax of the ip ospf bfd disable command is: ip ospf bfd disable.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route bfd command do?

The ip route bfd command enables or disables BFD on the specified static route. To disable BFD, issue the command without the bfd option. Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR> | <INTERFACE>] [bfd].
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route bfd command?
The syntax of the ip route bfd command is: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR> | <INTERFACE>] [bfd]
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command enables BFD for OSPFv3 on the current interface. The interface must have OSPFv3 enabled on it. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. The no form of this command sets the current interface to the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command is: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable command do?
The IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable command disables BFD on the current OSPFv3 interface. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable command is: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor fall-over bfd command do?

The neighbor fall-over bfd command enables BGP to register with BFD to receive fast peering session deactivation messages from BFD. The no form of this command disables BGP for BFD. NOTE: Currently, BFD is supported only with IPv4 neighbors. Support to enable BFD with IPv6 neighbors will be added in a future release. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} fall-over bfd.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor fall-over bfd command?
The syntax of the neighbor fall-over bfd command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} fall-over bfd.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show bfd command do?
The show bfd command shows information for all BFD sessions or for a specific BFD session. Syntax: show bfd [session <ID>] [all-vrfs | vrf <NAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show bfd command?
The syntax of the show bfd command is: show bfd [session <ID>] [all-vrfs | vrf <NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show hsc command do?
The show hsc command displays connection information for the remote controller. Syntax: show hsc.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show hsc command?
The syntax of the show hsc command is: show hsc.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command do?

The clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command removes the protection switching and triggers reversion both in revertive and non-revertive operation. This command will not change the configured revertive operation mode. Syntax: clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command?
The syntax of the clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command is: clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear erps statistics command do?
The clear erps statistics command this command clears the ERPS statistics for a ring or a ring instance. Syntax: clear erps statistics [ring <id>] [instance <id>].
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear erps statistics command?
The syntax of the clear erps statistics command is: clear erps statistics [ring <id>] [instance <id>]
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring command do?
The erps ring command this command creates an ERPS ring with a given ID. The no form of this command removes all the configurations of the ring, including instances. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring command?
The syntax of the erps ring command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command do?

The erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command this command configures the ERPS ring member port. An L2 interface in the switch is associated to one of the two member ports of an ERPS ring. In case of an interconnection node, only port0 is applicable for the sub-ring. The no form of this command removes the association of the ring port to the L2 interface on the switch. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> description command do?

The erps ring <ringid> description command this command adds descriptive information to help administrators and operators understand the purpose of a ring. 1-64 printable ASCII characters are allowed. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> description command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> description command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> guard-interval command do?

The erps ring <ringid> guard-interval command guard timer is used in nodes recovering from a local failure to avoid loops due to earlier Signal Fail (SF) messages that may be in the ring. The configuration specifies the guard timer duration in units of 10 ms. The timer period must be greater than the maximum expected forwarding delay in which an R-APS message traverses the entire ring. The default value is 50. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the guard interval and sets it to the default value of 50. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> guard-interval command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> guard-interval command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command do?

The erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command specifies hold-off interval in units of 100 ms. If specified, a defect is not reported immediately. Instead, the hold-off timer is started. On expiration of the timer, if the defect still exists, it is reported to protection switching. The default value for hold-off timer is 0. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the hold-off interval and sets it to the default value of 0. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance command on a common ERPS network, a physical ring can be configured with a single ERPS ring, and only one blocked port can be specified in the ring. When the ERPS ring is in normal state, the blocked port prohibits all service packets from passing through. As a result, all service data is transmitted through one path over the ERPS ring, and the other link on the blocked port becomes idle, leading to ineffective use of bandwidth. To improve link use efficiency, logical rings can be configured in the same physical ring in the ERPS multi- instance. A port may have different roles in different ERPS rings and different ERPS rings use different control VLANs. An ERPS ring must be configured with an ERP instance, and each ERP instance specifies a range of VLANs. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> control-vlan command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> control-vlan command this command adds a control-channel VLAN to a ring instance. In an ERPS ring, the control VLANshould be used only to forward RAPS PDUs and not service packets. All the devices in an ERPS ring instance must be configured with the same control VLAN, and different ERPS ring instances must use different control VLANs. The no form of this command removes the control-channel VLAN of the ring instance. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> control-vlan command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> control-vlan command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> description command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> description command this command adds descriptive information to help administrators and operators understand the purpose of a ring instance. 1-64 printable ASCII characters are allowed. The no form of this command removes the ring instance description. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> description command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> description command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> enable command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> enable command this configuration enables protection switching on the given instance of the ring. It is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables protection switching on the given instance of the ring. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> enable command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> enable command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protected-vlans command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protected-vlans command this command specifies the set of VLANs that are protected by this ring instance. The no form of this command removes a set of VLANs that are protected by this ring instance. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protected-vlans command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protected-vlans command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protection-switch command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protection-switch command blocks a specific ring interface in one of the two following ways: • Force: The switch blocks a specific ring interface regardless of the protection switching state of the ring instance. • Manual: The switch blocks a specific ring interface if no other protection switch event is active on the ring instance. NOTE: The user can verify whether the protection-switch is successful by verifying the status of instance and port state over which this command is executed. Syntax: erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protection-switch {{manual|force} <port0>|<port1>}.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protection-switch command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protection-switch command is: erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protection-switch {{manual|force} <port0>|<port1>}.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command configures the default revertive mode of operation for an ERPS ring. In revertive operation, after the conditions causing protection switching are cleared, traffic channels are restored to the recovered link blocking the RPL. This configuration is meaningful only on the RPL node. The no form of this command configures non-revertive mode of operation for an ERPS ring. In non-revertive operation, the traffic channels continue to use the RPL, if it has not failed, after conditions causing protection switching are cleared. This configuration is meaningful only on the RPL node. Syntax: erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command is: erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> role command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> role command in ERPS, there is a central node called RPL Owner Node which blocks one of the ports to ensure that there is no loop formed for the Ethernet traffic. The link blocked by the RPL owner node is called the Ring Protection Link or RPL. The node at the other end of the RPL is known as RPL Neighbor Node. It uses R-APS control messages to coordinate the activities of switching on/off the RPL link. This command specifies the role of the node as owner or neighbor. The no form of this command removes the configuration of the node role from the instance. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> role command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> role command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> rpl command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> rpl command in ERPS, there is a central node called RPL Owner Node which blocks one of the ports to ensure that there is no loop formed for the Ethernet traffic. The link blocked by the RPL owner node is called the Ring Protection Link or RPL. The node at the other end of the RPL is known as RPL Neighbor Node. It uses R-APS control messages to coordinate the activities of switching the RPL link on and off. This command specifies which of the ERPS ring ports is the RPL. The no form of this command removes the RPL port configuration from the ERPS ring instance. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> rpl command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> rpl command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> meg-level command do?

The erps ring <ringid> meg-level command the R-APS messages transmitted by ERPS take the form of OAM PDUs as defined in G.8013. Each OAM PDU is transmitted at a specified level known as the Maintenance Entity Group (MEG) level. This command configures the level with which the ERPS packets must be transmitted. The no form of this command removes the configured MEG level and sets it to the default value of 7. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> meg-level command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> meg-level command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> parent-ring command do?

The erps ring <ringid> parent-ring command this command associates a sub-ring to a parent-ring and is required for the sub-ring to notify the parent- ring on change in topology. The no form of this command removes the parent ring identifier. 56 AOS-CX 10.06 High Availability Guide. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> parent-ring command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> parent-ring command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> sub-ring command do?

The erps ring <ringid> sub-ring command this command is to configure a sub-ring. If not specified, the ring is a major-ring. The no form of this command removes the sub-ring configuration of the ring and configures it to be a major-ring. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> sub-ring command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> sub-ring command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command do?

The erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command this command is to configure a sub-ring interconnection node to pass a topology change notification to the ring instance for the parent ring whenever the topology of the sub-ring changes. The parent ring instance performs a Forwarding Database (FDB) flush and sends a protocol message to ensure that other nodes on the parent ring also perform an FDB flush. The no form of this command disables topology change notifications. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> transmission-interval command do?

The erps ring <ringid> transmission-interval command specifies the R-APS periodic transmission interval in units of seconds. Default is 5 seconds. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the transmission interval and sets it to the default value of 5 seconds. 58 AOS-CX 10.06 High Availability Guide. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> transmission-interval command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> transmission-interval command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> wtr-interval command do?

The erps ring <ringid> wtr-interval command the RPL owner node uses a delay timer before initiating an RPL block in case of both revertive mode of operation or before reverting to idle state after clearing operator commands (FS, MS). The Wait to Restore (WTR) timer can be configured in 1-minute increments up to 12 minutes. The default value is 5 minutes. When recovering from an SF, the delay timer must be long enough to allow the recovering network to become stable. In the default revertive mode of operation, the WTR timer is used to prevent frequent operation of protection switching due to intermittent SF defects. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the wtr-interval and sets it to the default value of 5 minutes. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> wtr-interval command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> wtr-interval command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show erps statistics command do?

The show erps statistics command this command displays ERPS statistics. The statistics can be displayed for the ring, the instance, or the instance ports. Syntax: show erps statistics [ring <ringid>] [instance <id> [port0|port1]].
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show erps statistics command?
The syntax of the show erps statistics command is: show erps statistics [ring <ringid>] [instance <id> [port0|port1]]
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show erps status command do?

The show erps status command this command displays detailed information about a specific ring or all instances of a ring. The ring instance may be in one of the following states: • • Idle: The ring instance is operational. Initializing: The ring instance is not operational. • Protection: Protection switching has been triggered by a local or remote link failure. • Pending: Pending clearance of a previous protection switch. • Down: Ring instance is not active. • Manual-switch: Manual protection switching triggered by Admin-down. • Force-switch: Forced protection switching triggered by admin. A ring instance has the following reasons for "down" state: . Syntax: show erps status [ring <ringid>] [instance <id>].
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show erps status command?
The syntax of the show erps status command is: show erps status [ring <ringid>] [instance <id>]
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show erps summary command do?
The show erps summary command this command displays a summary of the ERPS configuration and state for the ERPS ring instances. Syntax: show erps summary.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show erps summary command?
The syntax of the show erps summary command is: show erps summary.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what security policy information is documented for About security?

About security: This AOS-CX Switch provides the following security features: • Local user and group management. • Authentication, Authorization, and Accounting (AAA), either local (password or SSH public key-based), or remote password-based TACACS+ or RADIUS. • SSH server. SSH is a cryptographic protocol that encrypts all communication between devices. • Ability to use enhanced security as described in Configuring enhanced security. • Making sensitive switch configuration information available for secure export/import between switches. For information, see service export-password. About Authentication, Authorization, and Accounting (AAA) • Authentication: identifies users, validates their credentials, and grants switch access. • Authorization: controls authenticated users command execution and switch interaction privileges. • Accounting: collects and manages user session activity logs for auditing and reporting purposes. Local AAA on your Aruba switch provides: • Authentication using local password or SSH public key.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what security policy information is documented for Managing local users and groups?

Managing local users and groups: Default user admin A factory-default switch comes with a single user named admin. The admin user: • Has an empty password. Press Enter in response to the admin password prompt. At initial boot, you are prompted to define a password for the admin user. Although empty (blank) passwords are allowed, it is recommended that you use strong passwords for all production switches. • Is a member of the administrators group. • Cannot be removed from the switch. NOTE: The switch admin user is distinct from the Service OS admin user. The Service OS acts as the bootloader and recovery operating system. The Service OS has its own CLI.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the user <USERNAME> group {administrators | operators | auditors | <USER-GROUP>} command do?

The user <USERNAME> group {administrators | operators | auditors | <USER-GROUP>} command creates a user and adds the user to one of the user groups. Users are given the privileges of their group. For the three built-in user groups (administrators, operators, auditors), the privileges are fixed. For user- defined local user groups, the privileges are defined by the CLI command authorization rules of the group. When entered without either optional ciphertext or plaintext parameters, the cleartext password is prompted for twice, with the characters entered masked with "*" symbols. The no form of this command removes a user account from the switch. The administrator cannot delete the user account from which they are logged in. The admin user cannot be deleted. Syntax: user <USERNAME> group {administrators | operators | auditors | <USER-GROUP>}.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the user <USERNAME> group {administrators | operators | auditors | <USER-GROUP>} command?
The syntax of the user <USERNAME> group {administrators | operators | auditors | <USER-GROUP>} command is: user <USERNAME> group {administrators | operators | auditors | <USER-GROUP>}.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the user-group <GROUP-NAME> command do?

The user-group <GROUP-NAME> command if <GROUP-NAME> does not exist, this command creates a local user group and then enters its context. If <GROUP-NAME> exists, this command enters the context for the specified <GROUP-NAME>. Within the <GROUP-NAME> context, several subcommands are available for working with rules that specify what CLI commands are permitted or denied for all members of the local group. In addition to the three built-in user groups administrators, operators, and auditors, up to 29 user- defined local user groups can be defined. All users can be members of only one of the up to 32 groups. The no form of this command deletes the specified user group. All members of the deleted group lose all command authorization privilege. Syntax: user-group <GROUP-NAME>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the user-group <GROUP-NAME> command?
The syntax of the user-group <GROUP-NAME> command is: user-group <GROUP-NAME>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the user <USERNAME> password [ciphertext <CIPHERTEXT-PASSWORD> | plaintext <PLAINTEXT-PASSWORD>] command do?

The user <USERNAME> password [ciphertext <CIPHERTEXT-PASSWORD> | plaintext <PLAINTEXT-PASSWORD>] command changes a password for an account or enables the password for the admin account. When entered without either optional ciphertext or plaintext parameters, the cleartext password is prompted for twice, with the characters entered masked with "*" symbols. Syntax: user <USERNAME> password [ciphertext <CIPHERTEXT-PASSWORD> | plaintext <PLAINTEXT-PASSWORD>].
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the user <USERNAME> password [ciphertext <CIPHERTEXT-PASSWORD> | plaintext <PLAINTEXT-PASSWORD>] command?

The syntax of the user <USERNAME> password [ciphertext <CIPHERTEXT-PASSWORD> | plaintext <PLAINTEXT-PASSWORD>] command is: user <USERNAME> password [ciphertext <CIPHERTEXT-PASSWORD> | plaintext <PLAINTEXT-PASSWORD>]
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the service export-password command do?

The service export-password command configures a nondefault export password. The export password is used to transform critical security parameters (such as password hashes) into ciphertext suitable for exporting and showing by commands such as show running-config. This transformation enables safe switch configuration import and export. The no form of this command reverts the export password to its factory default. NOTE: All factory-default switches have identical default export passwords. For security, it is recommended that you set the same nondefault export password on every switch in a group that will exchange configuration information. Only switches with identical export passwords can exchange configuration information. 24. Syntax: service export-password.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the service export-password command?
The syntax of the service export-password command is: service export-password.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show user-group command do?

The show user-group command shows user group information for the built-in groups plus any user-defined local user groups. When entered without <GROUP-NAME>, summary information is shown for all groups. Syntax: show user-group [<GROUP-NAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show user-group command?
The syntax of the show user-group command is: show user-group [<GROUP-NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show user information command do?

The show user information command shows the following information for the logged-in user: • User name. • User authentication type: local, RADIUS, or TACACS+. • User group: administrators, operators, or <GROUP-NAME>. • User privilege level: For the built-in user groups and RADIUS or TACACS+, the role privilege level value is shown. For user-defined user groups, N/A is shown. Syntax: show user information.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show user information command?
The syntax of the show user information command is: show user information.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show user-list command do?
The show user-list command shows all configured users and their corresponding group names. Syntax: show user-list [vsx-peer].
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show user-list command?
The syntax of the show user-list command is: show user-list [vsx-peer]
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what security policy information is documented for SSH server?

SSH server: About the SSH server SSH (Secure Shell) is a cryptographic protocol that encrypts all communication between devices. Each switch VRF includes an SSH server. The SSH server on the mgmt VRF is enabled by default in software version 10.02 and higher, and disabled in version 10.01 and lower. Only the SSH servers included in the switch are supported. The SSH server provides SSH client to switch communications, enabling SSH clients (at least SSH v2.0) to connect to the switch for the purpose of managing it. The SSH server interfaces with the authentication service that provides local and/or remote AAA. NOTE: The SSH server will perform a rekey operation for all open SSH sessions at every hour or after 1 GB of data transferred, whichever occurs first. The rekey is performed to address a common security concern that encryption/decryption keys not be used for long periods of time. This limits the amount of data exposed in the unfortunate case where a key is exposed or refactored. NOTE: SSH public key authentication is separate from SSH server.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ssh host-key command do?
The show ssh host-key command shows the public host keys for the SSH server. If the key type is not provided, all available host-keys are shown. Syntax: show ssh host-key [ecdsa | ed25519 | rsa].
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ssh host-key command?
The syntax of the show ssh host-key command is: show ssh host-key [ecdsa | ed25519 | rsa]
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ssh server command?
The syntax of the show ssh server command is: show ssh server [vrf <VRF-NAME> | all-vrfs] [vsx-peer]
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ssh server sessions command do?

The show ssh server sessions command shows the active SSH sessions on a specified VRF or on all VRFs. If no VRF is specified, the active sessions on the default VRF are shown. Syntax: show ssh server sessions [vrf <VRF-NAME> | all-vrfs] [vsx-peer].
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ssh server sessions command?
The syntax of the show ssh server sessions command is: show ssh server sessions [vrf <VRF-NAME> | all-vrfs] [vsx-peer]
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ssh ciphers command do?

The ssh ciphers command configures SSH to use a set of ciphers in the specified priority order. Ciphers in SSH are used for privacy of data being transported over the connection. The first cipher type entered in the CLI is considered a first priority. Each option is an algorithm that is used to encrypt the link and each name indicates the algorithm and cryptographic parameters that are used. Only ciphers that are entered by the user are configured. The no form of this command removes the configuration of ciphers and reverts SSH to use the default set of ciphers. Syntax: ssh ciphers <CIPHERS-LIST>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ssh ciphers command?
The syntax of the ssh ciphers command is: ssh ciphers <CIPHERS-LIST>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ssh host-key-algorithms command do?

The ssh host-key-algorithms command configures SSH to use a set of host key algorithms in the specified priority order. Host key algorithms specify which host key types are allowed to be used for the SSH connection. The first host key entered in the CLI is considered a first priority. Each option represents a type of key that can be used. Host keys are used to verify the host that you are connecting to. This configuration allows you to control which host key types are presented to incoming clients, or which host key types to receive first from hosts. Only the host key algorithms that are specified by the user are configured. The no form of this command removes the configuration of host key algorithms and reverts SSH to use the default set of algorithms. Syntax: ssh host-key-algorithms <HOST-KEY-ALGORITHMS-LIST>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ssh host-key-algorithms command?
The syntax of the ssh host-key-algorithms command is: ssh host-key-algorithms <HOST-KEY-ALGORITHMS-LIST>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ssh key-exchange-algorithms command do?

The ssh key-exchange-algorithms command configures SSH to use a set of key exchange algorithm types in the specified priority order. The first key exchange type entered in the CLI is considered a first priority. Key exchange algorithms are used to exchange a shared session key with a peer securely. Each option represents an algorithm that is used to distribute a shared key in a way that prevents outside interference, manipulation, or recovery. Only the key exchange algorithms that are specified by the user are configured. The no form of this command removes the configuration of key exchange algorithms and reverts SSH to use the default set of algorithms. Syntax: ssh key-exchange-algorithms <KEY-EXCHANGE-ALGORITHMS-LIST>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ssh key-exchange-algorithms command?
The syntax of the ssh key-exchange-algorithms command is: ssh key-exchange-algorithms <KEY-EXCHANGE-ALGORITHMS-LIST>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ssh known-host remove command do?

The ssh known-host remove command clears the list of trusted SSH servers for your user account. When you download or upload a file to or from a server using SFTP, you establish a trusted SSH relationship with that server. Each user account maintains its own set of SSH server host-keys for every server to which the user previously connected. Syntax: ssh known-host remove {all | {<IPv4-ADDRESS> | <HOSTNAME> | <IPv6-ADDRESS>} }.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ssh known-host remove command?
The syntax of the ssh known-host remove command is: ssh known-host remove {all | {<IPv4-ADDRESS> | <HOSTNAME> | <IPv6-ADDRESS>} }.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ssh macs command do?

The ssh macs command configures SSH to use a set of message authentication codes (MAC s) in the specified priority order. The first MAC entered in the CLI is considered a first priority. MAC s maintain the integrity of each message sent across an SSH connection. Each option represents an algorithm that can be used to provide integrity between peers. Only the MAC types that are specified by the user are configured. The no form of this command removes the configuration of MAC s and reverts SSH to use the default set of MAC s. Syntax: ssh macs <MACS-LIST>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ssh macs command?
The syntax of the ssh macs command is: ssh macs <MACS-LIST>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ssh maximum-auth-attempts command do?

The ssh maximum-auth-attempts command sets the SSH maximum number of authentication attempts. The no form of the command resets the maximum to its default of 6. Syntax: ssh maximum-auth-attempts <ATTEMPTS>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ssh maximum-auth-attempts command?
The syntax of the ssh maximum-auth-attempts command is: ssh maximum-auth-attempts <ATTEMPTS>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ssh public-key-algorithms command do?

The ssh public-key-algorithms command configures SSH to use a set of public key algorithms in the specified priority order. The first public key type entered in the CLI is considered a first priority. Public key algorithms specify which public key types can be used for public key authentication in SSH. Each option represents a public key type that the SSH server can accept or that the SSH client can present to a server. Only the public key algorithms that are chosen by the user are configured. The no form of this command removes the configuration of public key algorithms and reverts SSH to use the default set. Syntax: ssh public-key-algorithms <PUBLIC-KEY-ALGORITHMS-LIST>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ssh public-key-algorithms command?
The syntax of the ssh public-key-algorithms command is: ssh public-key-algorithms <PUBLIC-KEY-ALGORITHMS-LIST>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ssh server vrf command do?
The ssh server vrf command enables the SSH server on the specified VRF. The no form of the command disables the SSH server on the specified VRF. Syntax: ssh server vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ssh server vrf command?
The syntax of the ssh server vrf command is: ssh server vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ssh (client login) command do?
The ssh (client login) command establishes a client session with an SSH server which is typically another switch. Syntax: ssh [<USERNAME>@]{<IPv4> | <HOSTNAME>} [vrf <VRF-NAME>] [port <PORT-NUMBER>].
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ssh (client login) command?
The syntax of the ssh (client login) command is: ssh [<USERNAME>@]{<IPv4> | <HOSTNAME>} [vrf <VRF-NAME>] [port <PORT-NUMBER>]
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what security policy information is documented for Local AAA?

Local AAA: About local AAA Local AAA on your Aruba switch provides: • Authentication using local password or SSH public key. • Authorization using local role-based access control (RBAC). Optional per-command authorization is possible through configuration of user-defined local user groups, with command authorization rules applied to respective group members. • Accounting of user activity on the switch using accounting logs. NOTE: For switches that support multiple management modules such as the Aruba 8400, all AAA functionality discussed only applies to the active management module. See also AAA on switches with multiple management modules in the High Availability Guide. Local AAA defaults and limits Setting Default value / limit Local authentication Enabled by default for all connection types: console, SSH, and REST. Local role-based access control (RBAC) authorization Enabled by default for all connection types: console, SSH, and REST. Local accounting Enabled. Maximum number of local users 64 users, including the default admin user.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa accounting all-mgmt command do?

The aaa accounting all-mgmt command defines accounting as being local (with the name local) (the default). Or defines a sequence of remote AAA server groups to be accessed for accounting purposes. For remote accounting, the information is sent to the first reachable remote server that was configured with this command for remote accounting. If no remote server is reachable, local accounting remains available. Each available connection type (channel) can be configured individually as either local or using remote AAA server groups. All server groups named in your command, must exist. This command can be issued multiple times, once for each connection type. Local is always available for any connection type not configured for remote accounting. Syntax: aaa accounting all-mgmt <CONNECTION-TYPE> start-stop {local | group <GROUP-LIST>}.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the aaa accounting all-mgmt command?
The syntax of the aaa accounting all-mgmt command is: aaa accounting all-mgmt <CONNECTION-TYPE> start-stop {local | group <GROUP-LIST>}.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authentication limit-login-attempts command do?

The aaa authentication limit-login-attempts command enables local login attempt limiting. If the number of failed local login attempts equals the configured threshold, the user is locked out for the configured duration. The no form of this command disables local login attempt limits. NOTE: This local login attempt limiting feature is only available when not using remote authentication through AAA servers (TACACS+ or RADIUS). Syntax: aaa authentication limit-login-attempts <ATTEMPTS> lockout-time <LOCKOUT-TIME>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication limit-login-attempts command?
The syntax of the aaa authentication limit-login-attempts command is: aaa authentication limit-login-attempts <ATTEMPTS> lockout-time <LOCKOUT-TIME>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authentication login command do?

The aaa authentication login command defines authentication as being local (with the name local) (the default). Or defines a sequence of remote AAA server groups to be accessed for authentication purposes. Each available connection type (channel) can be configured individually as either local or using remote AAA server groups. All server groups named in your command, must exist. This command can be issued multiple times, once for each connection type. Local is always available for any connection type not configured for remote AAA authentication. The no form of this command removes for the specified connection type, any defined remote AAA server group authentication sequence. Syntax: aaa authentication login <CONNECTION-TYPE> {local | group <GROUP-LIST>}.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication login command?
The syntax of the aaa authentication login command is: aaa authentication login <CONNECTION-TYPE> {local | group <GROUP-LIST>}.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authentication minimum-password-length command do?

The aaa authentication minimum-password-length command enables minimum password length checking. Existing passwords shorter than the minimum length are unaffected. Length checking does not apply to ciphertext passwords. Length checking applies both to local and remote authentication. The no form of this command disables minimum password length checking. Syntax: aaa authentication minimum-password-length <LENGTH>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication minimum-password-length command?
The syntax of the aaa authentication minimum-password-length command is: aaa authentication minimum-password-length <LENGTH>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authorization commands command do?

The aaa authorization commands command defines authorization as being basic local RBAC (specified as none), or as full-fledged local RBAC specified as local (the default), or as remote TACACS+ (specified with group <GROUP-LIST>). Each available connection type (channel) can be configured individually. All server groups named in the command, must exist. This command can be issued multiple times, once for each connection type. The no form of this command unconfigures authorization for the specified connection type, reverting to the default of local. NOTE: Although only TACACS+ servers are supported for remote authorization, local authorization (basic or full-fledged) can be used with remote RADIUS authentication. Syntax: aaa authorization commands <CONNECTION-TYPE> {local | none}.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the aaa authorization commands command?
The syntax of the aaa authorization commands command is: aaa authorization commands <CONNECTION-TYPE> {local | none}.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show aaa accounting command do?
The show aaa accounting command shows the accounting configuration per connection type (channel). Syntax: show aaa accounting [vsx-peer].
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show aaa accounting command?
The syntax of the show aaa accounting command is: show aaa accounting [vsx-peer]
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show aaa authentication command do?
The show aaa authentication command is documented in this guide. Syntax: show aaa authentication [vsx-peer].
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show aaa authentication command?
The syntax of the show aaa authentication command is: show aaa authentication [vsx-peer]
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show aaa authorization command do?
The show aaa authorization command shows the authorization configuration per connection type (channel). Syntax: show aaa authorization [vsx-peer].
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show aaa authorization command?
The syntax of the show aaa authorization command is: show aaa authorization [vsx-peer]
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ssh authentication-method command do?

The show ssh authentication-method command shows the status of the SSH public key method and the local password-based (through SSH client) authentication method. Syntax: show ssh authentication-method.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ssh authentication-method command?
The syntax of the show ssh authentication-method command is: show ssh authentication-method.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show user command do?
The show user command shows the SSH client public key list for a specified user. Syntax: show user <USERNAME> authorized-key.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show user command?
The syntax of the show user command is: show user <USERNAME> authorized-key.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ssh password-authentication command do?

The ssh password-authentication command enables the password-based authentication method for use with SSH clients. The no form of this command disables the password-based authentication method for use with SSH clients. Syntax: ssh password-authentication.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ssh password-authentication command?
The syntax of the ssh password-authentication command is: ssh password-authentication.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ssh public-key-authentication command do?

The ssh public-key-authentication command enables the SSH public key authentication method. The switch ships with SSH public key authentication enabled. The no form of this command disables the SSH public key authentication method. NOTE: Although SSH public key authentication is enabled by default, it cannot be used until SSH public keys are added with the user authorized-key command. Syntax: ssh public-key-authentication.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ssh public-key-authentication command?
The syntax of the ssh public-key-authentication command is: ssh public-key-authentication.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the user <USERNAME> authorized-key <PUBKEY> command do?

The user <USERNAME> authorized-key <PUBKEY> command copies an SSH client public key into the key list. If the key list and the public key do not exist, it creates a list with the public key. If the SSH client public key exists, the command appends the new key to the existing list. The client public key list holds a maximum of 32 client keys. The no form of the command removes either one or all SSH public keys from the key list. Syntax: user <USERNAME> authorized-key <PUBKEY>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the user <USERNAME> authorized-key <PUBKEY> command?
The syntax of the user <USERNAME> authorized-key <PUBKEY> command is: user <USERNAME> authorized-key <PUBKEY>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what security policy information is documented for Remote AAA with TACACS+?

Remote AAA with TACACS+: About remote AAA with TACACS+ Remote AAA provides the following for your Aruba switch: • Authentication using remote TACACS+ AAA servers. • Authorization using remote TACACS+ AAA servers, providing fine-grained command authorization. Optional user-defined local user groups with configured command authorization rules can be used to provide authorization fallback protection for when TACACS+ servers become temporarily unavailable. • Transmission of locally collected accounting information to remote TACACS+ servers. NOTE: For switches that support multiple management modules such as the Aruba 8400, all AAA functionality discussed only applies to the active management module. See also AAA on switches with multiple management modules in the High Availability Guide. Default server groups The switch always has these four default groups: • tacacs: for remote AAA, always contains every configured TACACS+ server. • radius: for remote AAA, always contains every configured RADIUS server. • local: for local authentication. • none: for local (RBAC) authorization.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what security policy information is documented for Remote AAA with RADIUS?

Remote AAA with RADIUS: About remote AAA with RADIUS Remote AAA provides the following for your Aruba switch: • Authentication using remote RADIUS AAA servers. For added security, two-factor authentication may be used. In two-factor authentication, X.509 certificate-based authentication is combined with RADIUS authentication. • Command authorization is not supported by RADIUS servers, however, user-defined local user groups can be configured with command-authorization rules, providing locally configured per-command authorization for members of such groups. See User-defined user groups. In the switch default state (without user-defined local groups), basic role-based authorization is available with the three built-in roles (administrators, operators, auditors). • Transmission of locally collected accounting information to remote RADIUS servers. NOTE: For switches that support multiple management modules such as the Aruba 8400, all AAA functionality discussed only applies to the active management module. See also AAA on switches with multiple management modules in the High Availability Guide.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what security policy information is documented for Remote AAA (TACACS+, RADIUS) commands?

Remote AAA (TACACS+, RADIUS) commands: aaa accounting all-mgmt Syntax aaa accounting all-mgmt <CONNECTION-TYPE> start-stop {local | group <GROUP-LIST>} no aaa accounting all-mgmt <CONNECTION-TYPE> Description Defines accounting as being local (with the name local) (the default). Or defines a sequence of remote AAA server groups to be accessed for accounting purposes. For remote accounting, the information is sent to the first reachable remote server that was configured with this command for remote accounting. If no remote server is reachable, local accounting remains available. Each available connection type (channel) can be configured individually as either local or using remote AAA server groups. All server groups named in your command, must exist. This command can be issued multiple times, once for each connection type. Local is always available for any connection type not configured for remote accounting. NOTE: The system accounting log is not associated with any connection type (channel) and is therefore sent to the accounting method configured on the default connection type (channel) only.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa authentication allow-fail-through command do?

The aaa authentication allow-fail-through command enables authentication fail-through. When this option is enabled, the next server/authentication method is tried after an authentication failure. The no form of this command disables authentication fail-through. If the system fails to authenticate with a reachable TACACS+ or RADIUS server, the system does not attempt to authenticate with the next TACACS+/ RADIUS server. Syntax: aaa authentication allow-fail-through.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the aaa authentication allow-fail-through command?
The syntax of the aaa authentication allow-fail-through command is: aaa authentication allow-fail-through.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aaa group server command do?

The aaa group server command creates an AAA server group that is either empty or contains preconfigured RADIUS/TACACS+ servers. You can create a maximum of 28 server groups. The no form of this command deletes a server group. Only a preconfigured user-defined RADIUS/TACACS+ server group can be deleted. RADIUS or TACACS+ servers that were in a deleted server group remain a part of their default server group. The default server group for TACACS+ servers is tacacs. The default server group for RADIUS servers is radius. Syntax: aaa group server {tacacs | radius} <SERVER-GROUP-NAME>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the aaa group server command?
The syntax of the aaa group server command is: aaa group server {tacacs | radius} <SERVER-GROUP-NAME>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the radius-server auth-type command do?

The radius-server auth-type command enables the CHAP or PAP authentication protocol, which is used for communication with the RADIUS servers, at the global level. You can override this command with a fine-grained per server auth-type configuration. The no form of this command resets the global authentication mechanism for RADIUS to PAP, which is the default authentication mechanism for RADIUS. Syntax: radius-server auth-type {pap | chap}.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the radius-server auth-type command?
The syntax of the radius-server auth-type command is: radius-server auth-type {pap | chap}.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the radius-server key command do?

The radius-server key command creates or modifies a RADIUS global passkey. The RADIUS global passkey is used as a shared-secret for encrypting the communication between all RADIUS servers and the switch. The RADIUS global passkey is required for authentication unless local passkeys have been set. By default, the RADIUS global passkey is empty. If the administrator has not set this key, the switch will not be able to perform RADIUS authentication. The switch will instead rely on the authentication mechanism configured with aaa authentication login. The no form of the command removes the global passkey. Syntax: radius-server key {plaintext <GLOBAL-PASSKEY> | ciphertext <GLOBAL-PASSKEY>}.

The syntax of the radius-server key command is: radius-server key {plaintext <GLOBAL-PASSKEY> | ciphertext <GLOBAL-PASSKEY>}.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the radius-server retries command do?

The radius-server retries command sets at the global level the number of retries the switch makes before concluding that the RADIUS server is unreachable. You can override this setting with a fine-grained per RADIUS server retries configuration. See radius- server host. The no form of this command resets the RADIUS global retries to the default retries value of 1. Syntax: radius-server retries <0-5>.
