For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmp system command do?
The show snmp system command displays SNMP description, location, and contact information. Syntax: show snmp system.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmp system command?
The syntax of the show snmp system command is: show snmp system.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmp trap command do?
The show snmp trap command displays all configured SNMP traps/informs receivers. Syntax: show snmp trap.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmp trap command?
The syntax of the show snmp trap command is: show snmp trap.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmp vrf command do?
The show snmp vrf command displays the VRF on which the SNMP agent service is running. Syntax: show snmp vrf.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmp vrf command?
The syntax of the show snmp vrf command is: show snmp vrf.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmpv3 context command do?
The show snmpv3 context command displays all configured SNMP contexts. Syntax: show snmpv3 context.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 context command?
The syntax of the show snmpv3 context command is: show snmpv3 context.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmpv3 engine-id command do?

The show snmpv3 engine-id command displays the configured SNMP v3 snmp engine-id. If the SNMP v3 engine-id is not configured, by default a unique engine-id is created by the switch using a combination of the enterprise OID value and the switch's MAC address. Syntax: show snmpv3 engine-id.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 engine-id command?
The syntax of the show snmpv3 engine-id command is: show snmpv3 engine-id.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmpv3 security-level command do?
The show snmpv3 security-level command displays the configured SNMP v3 security level. Syntax: show snmpv3 security-level.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 security-level command?
The syntax of the show snmpv3 security-level command is: show snmpv3 security-level.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show snmpv3 users command do?
The show snmpv3 users command displays all configured SNMP v3 users. For more details on the user enabled status, see snmpv3 security-level. Syntax: show snmpv3 users.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show snmpv3 users command?
The syntax of the show snmpv3 users command is: show snmpv3 users.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server agent-port command do?

The snmp-server agent-port command sets the UDP port number that the SNMP master agent uses to communicate. UDP port 161 is the default port. The no form of this command sets the SNMP master agent port to the default value. Syntax: snmp-server agent-port <PORT>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server agent-port command?
The syntax of the snmp-server agent-port command is: snmp-server agent-port <PORT>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server community command do?

The snmp-server community command adds an SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. A community string is a password that controls read access to the SNMP agent. A network management program must supply this name when attempting to get SNMP information from the switch. A maximum of 10 community strings are supported. Once you create your own community string, the default community string (public) is deleted. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. When no community string exists, a default community string with the value public is automatically defined. Syntax: snmp-server community <STRING>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server community command?
The syntax of the snmp-server community command is: snmp-server community <STRING>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server historical-counters-monitor command do?

The snmp-server historical-counters-monitor command enables the Remote Network Monitoring agent (rmond) to start collecting historical interface statistics. The no form of this command stops the historical interface statistics collection. Syntax: snmp-server historical-counters-monitor.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server historical-counters-monitor command?
The syntax of the snmp-server historical-counters-monitor command is: snmp-server historical-counters-monitor.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server host command do?
The snmp-server host command is documented in this guide. Syntax: snmp-server host <IPv4-ADDR | IPv6-ADDR> trap version <VERSION> [community <STRING>].
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server host command?
The syntax of the snmp-server host command is: snmp-server host <IPv4-ADDR | IPv6-ADDR> trap version <VERSION> [community <STRING>]
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server system-contact command do?
The snmp-server system-contact command sets SNMP contact information. The no form of this command removes the SNMP contact information. Syntax: snmp-server system-contact <INFO>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server system-contact command?
The syntax of the snmp-server system-contact command is: snmp-server system-contact <INFO>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server system-location command do?
The snmp-server system-location command sets the SNMP location information. The no form of this command removes the SNMP location information. Syntax: snmp-server system-location <INFO>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server system-location command?
The syntax of the snmp-server system-location command is: snmp-server system-location <INFO>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmp-server vrf command do?

The snmp-server vrf command configures a VRF on which the SNMP agent listens for incoming requests. By default, the SNMP agent does not listen on any VRF. 6100 only supports default VRF. The SNMP agent can listen on multiple VRFs. The no form of this command stops the SNMP agent from listening for incoming requests on the specified VRF. Syntax: snmp-server vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server vrf command?
The syntax of the snmp-server vrf command is: snmp-server vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmp-server trap-source interface vrf command?
The syntax of the snmp-server trap-source interface vrf command is: snmp-server trap-source {interface <IF-NAME> | <IPv4-Address> | <IPv6-Address>} [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID> command do?

The snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID> command configures the SNMP v3 SNMP engine-id allowing an administrator to configure a unique SNMP engine-id for the switch. This engine-id is used by the NMS management tool to identify and distinguish multiple switches on the same network. The no form of this command restores the default engine-id, created by the switch using a combination of the enterprise OID value and the switch's MAC address. Configures the SNMP v3 security level. The security level determines which SMNPv3 users defined by the command snmpv3 user are able to connect. The no form of this command changes the security level as follows: 24 AOS-CX 10.06 SNMP/MIB Guide • no snmpv3 security-level auth: Sets the security level to auth-privacy. Syntax: snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID> command?
The syntax of the snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID> command is: snmpv3 engine-id <ENGINE-ID>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Entity MIB support?

Entity MIB support: The Entity MIB, rfc 6933, allows network managers to retrieve physical containment and logical relationships for devices in the network. The entconfig Change trap is sent to configured SNMP-server hosts when a change occurs. The trap is configured to send notifications no more than once every 5 seconds. We will be supporting the Entity MIB for read-only. Physical components that are supported include: • Chassis • • • • Fabric cards Fan trays Fans Line cards and their interfaces.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the Interface state command do?

The Interface state command a - Active An active LACP interface. C - Collecting Data frames are received through the aggregate link and sent onto the intended destination. D - Distributing Data frames are transmitted through the aggregate link to reach the intended destination. F - Aggregable The link can be used as part of an aggregate. E - Default neighbor state The link has the default state of the neighbor switch. I - Individual The link is used as an individual link. L - Long-timeout With the long timeout, an LACPDU is sent every 30 seconds. If no response comes from its partner after three LACPDUs are sent (90 seconds), a timeout event occurs. The LACP state machine then transitions to the appropriate state based on its current state.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Configuration consistency requirements?

Configuration consistency requirements: • Configure at least one active mode aggregation in two devices. • • For a successful static aggregation, make sure the ports at both ends of each link are in the same aggregation state. For a successful dynamic aggregation, make sure the peer ports of the ports aggregated at one end are also aggregated, and that one of the ends is configured as "active". The two ends can automatically negotiate the aggregation state of each member port. Removing interfaces • Deleting an aggregate interface also deletes its aggregation group and causes all member ports to leave the aggregation group. • When a member interface is removed from a LAG: ◦ 6100, 6200, 6300, and 6400 switches: The interface goes to its default status of unshut. ◦ 8320, 8325, 8360, or 8400 switches: The interface becomes disabled. Disabling an interface When an interface LAG is disabled with the shutdown command, all its members also become operationally down. Layer 2 aggregation groups All switches support static and dynamic layer 2 aggregation groups.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface lag command do?
The interface lag command creates a Link Aggregation Group (LAG) interface represented by an ID. The no form of this command deletes a LAG interface represented by an ID. Syntax: interface lag <ID>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface lag command?
The syntax of the interface lag command is: interface lag <ID>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip address command do?

The ip address command sets an IPv4 address and subnet mask to a LAG interface. One primary and up to 31 secondary address can be configured per interface. The no form of this command removes the IPv4 address from the interface. Syntax: ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [secondary].
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip address command?
The syntax of the ip address command is: ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [secondary]
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 address command do?
The IPv6 address command sets an IPv6 address and subnet mask to a LAG interface. The no form of this command removes the IPv6 address from the interface. Syntax: IPv6 address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 address command?
The syntax of the IPv6 address command is: IPv6 address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lacp hash [l2-src-dst | l3-src-dst | l4-src-dst] command do?

The lacp hash [l2-src-dst | l3-src-dst | l4-src-dst] command this command controls the selection of an interface in a group of aggregate interfaces. The hash type value helps transmit a frame. This configuration must be done at the global level. Sets an LACP mode to active or passive. The no form of this command sets the LACP mode to off, returning the LAG to a static mode aggregation. Sets the LACP port ID value of the member interface of the LAG. The no form of this command removes the LACP port ID value from the interface. Syntax: lacp hash [l2-src-dst | l3-src-dst | l4-src-dst].
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the lacp hash [l2-src-dst | l3-src-dst | l4-src-dst] command?
The syntax of the lacp hash [l2-src-dst | l3-src-dst | l4-src-dst] command is: lacp hash [l2-src-dst | l3-src-dst | l4-src-dst]
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lacp port-priority <PORT-PRIORITY> command do?

The lacp port-priority <PORT-PRIORITY> command sets an LACP port priority value for the member interface of the LAG. The no form of this command reverts the LACP port priority to the default, which is 1. Sets an LACP heartbeat request time to fast or slow. 40 AOS-CX 10.06 Link Aggregation Guide The no form of the command sets an LACP rate to slow. Sets a Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP) system priority. The no form of this command sets an LACP system priority to the default, which is 65534. Syntax: lacp port-priority <PORT-PRIORITY>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the lacp port-priority <PORT-PRIORITY> command?
The syntax of the lacp port-priority <PORT-PRIORITY> command is: lacp port-priority <PORT-PRIORITY>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan trunk native [<VLAN-ID>] command do?

The no vlan trunk native [<VLAN-ID>] command assigns a native VLAN ID to a LAG interface. The no form of this command removes a native VLAN from a LAG interface and assigns VLAN ID 1 as its native VLAN.

messages.content: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the The agent class constructor must begin with the following declaration: command do?

The The agent class constructor must begin with the following declaration: command describes what the script monitors. Must be a text string. Version Specifies version information about the script. The version is appended to the script name. The version must be a text string that starts with a letter or number and can contain alphanumeric characters and the special characters. (dot), - (hyphen), and _ (underscore). The maximum length of the name plus the version must not exceed 80 characters. Version must not be empty. Author Specifies the author of the script. Must be a text string. Optional items APIVersion Specifies which NAE script API version is required to run this script. This item is informational only. No validation or action is performed for this item. Syntax: The agent class constructor must begin with the following declaration: .

messages.content: For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the Max Algorithm(continuous_learning_window="<duration>") command do?

The Max Algorithm(continuous_learning_window="<duration>") command the Max Algorithm function smooths raw data within the initial learning time or the continuous learning window by calculating an average over time to find the greatest normal data during the past learning window. Syntax: Max Algorithm(continuous_learning_window="<duration>").
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the Max Algorithm(continuous_learning_window="<duration>") command?
The syntax of the Max Algorithm(continuous_learning_window="<duration>") command is: Max Algorithm(continuous_learning_window="<duration>")
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about algorithm for dynamic Threshold calculation?

algorithm for dynamic Threshold calculation: self.alg = Max Algorithm(continuous_learning_window="2h") More information How the Max Algorithm function works How the Max Algorithm function works The Max Algorithm function uses smoothed data over time to find the maximum value and calculate a baseline of "typical" data. By setting a high threshold based on this calculation, the number of false positives can be reduced. The agent generates alerts for extreme anomalies only. The Max Algorithm function is best for data that has no limit on its maximum value, such as incoming network traffic. Smoothing data The Max Algorithm function uses the "simple moving average" algorithm to smooth data. In general, data smoothing is the process of detecting and removing "noise" data, allowing important patterns to remain. In its formula, the simple moving average algorithm reduces the influence of datapoints that are not similar to the others because it includes multiple datapoints in the calculation.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the Inside a callback action: command do?

The Inside a callback action: command the CLI action executes the specified command from the switch CLI. Generates a multiple line report with customized content. The report content can be viewed in the Web UI from the alert details for the agent. The SHELL action executes the specified command in the Bash shell of the underlying Linux operating system of the switch. 124 AOS-CX 10.06 Network Analytics Engine Guide The Action Syslog action enters the specified message in the event log. Users can access event log messages from the CLI, REST API, or Web UI of the switch. Syntax: Inside a callback action: .
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the Inside a callback action: command?
The syntax of the Inside a callback action: command is: Inside a callback action:
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear nae-data command do?
The clear nae-data command clears all data collected by the Aruba Network Analytics Engine. Syntax: clear nae-data.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear nae-data command?
The syntax of the clear nae-data command is: clear nae-data.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show nae-agent command do?
The show nae-agent command shows information about the Aruba Network Analytics Engine agents that are available on the switch. Syntax: show nae-agent [vsx-peer].
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show nae-agent command?
The syntax of the show nae-agent command is: show nae-agent [vsx-peer]
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the https-server rest access-mode command do?
The https-server rest access-mode command changes the REST API access mode. The default mode is read-write. Syntax: https-server rest access-mode {read-only | read-write}.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the https-server rest access-mode command?
The syntax of the https-server rest access-mode command is: https-server rest access-mode {read-only | read-write}.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the https-server session close all command do?

The https-server session close all command invalidates and closes all HTTPS sessions. All existing Web UI and REST sessions are logged out and all real- time notification feature Web Socket connections are closed. Syntax: https-server session close all.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the https-server session close all command?
The syntax of the https-server session close all command is: https-server session close all.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the https-server vrf command do?

The https-server vrf command configures and starts the HTTPS server on the specified VRF. HTTPS server features include the REST API and the web user interfaces. The no form of the command stops any HTTPS servers running on the specified VRF and removes the HTTPS server configuration. Syntax: https-server vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the https-server vrf command?
The syntax of the https-server vrf command is: https-server vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show https-server command do?

The show https-server command shows the status and configuration of the HTTPS server. The REST API and web user interface are accessible only on VRFs that have the HTTPS server features configured. Syntax: show https-server [vsx-peer].
For 8400 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show https-server command?
The syntax of the show https-server command is: show https-server [vsx-peer]

- UMM - Unassigned Management Module:
- LC -Line card For event logs generated during an early boot or when the module role is not determined by the chassis switch, the module role will be set to UMM. After the module role is determined, the event logs are updated with AMM or SMM. Listed below are the module roles and its descriptions generated by the event log messages for a VSF stack:
- CDTR-Conductor - STBY-Standby - MMBR-Member - UKWN-Unknown For event logs generated during VSF discovery or when the VSF role is not determined by the VSF stack, the module role will be set to UKWN. After the VSF role is determined, the event logs are updated either with CDTR, STBY, or MMBR.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Shows information for each VLAN and the status of t?

Shows information for each VLAN and the status of t: he VLANs. Allows you to add, edit, and delete VLANs. Shows the information and the up or down status of the LAGs. Allows you to add, edit, and delete Lags. Shows user roles and names. Also allows you to add or delete a user and change user passwords. Administrator rights are required to access this sele ction.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.17.1000, what does the guide say about Security - PKI?

Security - PKI: Enables you to configure the switch to distribute fir mware to other switches. Enables you to run the ping command with variou s options. Enables you to run the traceroute command w ith various options. Enables you to run the showtech command. Ad ministrator rights are required to run this command. Shows the details of the spanning tree configuration, assigned ports, and inconsistent ports. Allows you to enable Spanning Tree with Multiple Spanning Tree or Rapid Per-VLANspanning Tree mode. Shows the details of the devices connected to the sw itch. Shows the details of the TA profile, EST profile, digi tal certificates, and associated applications in the switch. Allows you to add, edit, and delete TA profile; ad d, view, and delete EST profile; add, view, upload, and delete certificates; and edit associated applications.
